Wider Sports News.title: ITV ‘agrees’ 2027 Rugby World Cup rights deal
Wider Sports News.summary: Commercial network beats out BBC to show tournament on FTA television. The post ITV ‘agrees’ 2027 Rugby World Cup rights deal appeared first on SportsPro .
Wider Sports News.body:
Commercial broadcaster ITV has reportedly secured the UK live rights to the 2027 men’s Rugby World Cup, beating the BBC to the tournament, according to The Guardian. The BBC had explored a bid after strong viewing figures for last year’s Women’s Rugby World Cup. ITV, however, reportedly pushed for exclusive rather than shared rights, reflecting the strength of the advertising market around live rugby.

As a result, ITV now holds the rights to the major men’s international competitions through at least 2029. The free-to-air (FTA) broadcaster secured the rights to the first two editions of the Nations Championship in 2026 and 2028 in December last year, while it will continue to deliver every Six Nations match for the next three years.
Wider Sports News.copy:
Commercial broadcaster ITV has reportedly secured the UK live rights to the 2027 men’s Rugby World Cup, beating the BBC to the tournament, according to The Guardian. ITV, however, reportedly pushed for exclusive rather than shared rights, reflecting the strength of the advertising market around live rugby.

As a result, ITV now holds the rights to the major men’s international competitions through at least 2029. The free-to-air (FTA) broadcaster secured the rights to the first two editions of the Nations Championship in 2026 and 2028 in December last year, while it will continue to deliver every Six Nations match for the next three years.

Wider Sports News.title: HSBC strengthens global sports portfolio with China Open presenting partnership
Wider Sports News.summary: HSBC strengthens global sports portfolio with China Open presenting partnership SportsMint Media
Wider Sports News.body:
HSBC has expanded its investment in global sports sponsorship after becoming the presenting partner of the China Open, adding one of Asia’s premier tennis tournaments to a growing portfolio of high-profile sporting properties.

The long-term agreement will see the global banking group become a leading commercial partner of the combined ATP 500 and WTA 1000 event from the 2026 edition, reinforcing both HSBC’s commitment to the Chinese market and the tournament’s ambitions to elevate its international profile. Financial terms of the partnership were not disclosed.

Wider Sports News.title: The business of 26.2 miles: Inside marathon running’s commercial boom
Wider Sports News.summary: Marathon running has become one of sport's most valuable assets. Hans-Peter Zurbruegg, senior vice president of Active Lifestyle at Infront discusses how cities, sponsors and organisers are trying to sustain the boom. The post The business of 26.2 miles: Inside marathon running’s commercial boom appeared first on SportsPro .
Wider Sports News.body:
For much of the modern sporting era, commercial success was measured by audiences. The biggest events attracted the largest crowds, the highest television ratings and the most valuable sponsorships.

Marathon running has quietly built a different model. Across the world, major races are attracting unprecedented demand. Entry ballots are oversubscribed, participants are travelling across continents to secure places and cities are increasingly viewing their marathons as strategic assets rather than annual sporting events. What was once seen primarily as a test of personal endurance has evolved into a platform that generates value far beyond race day.

Wider Sports News.title: What does Comcast split mean for NBC, Sky and sports broadcasting in the UK and US?
Wider Sports News.summary: Comcast is dividing its communications and media businesses into two separate companies. SportsPro explains what this means for the future of sports broadcasting either side of the Atlantic and the industry as a whole. The post What does Comcast split mean for NBC, Sky and sports broadcasting in the UK and US? appeared first on SportsPro .
Wider Sports News.body:
US media giant Comcast’s decision to separate its content and broadcasting division from its cable and broadband business is the latest in a series of recent transactions and restructures within the industry designed to reflect changing consumption habits and the disruption caused by streaming.

The split will take a year to complete, after which NBCUniversal and Comcast operate as legally separate, publicly listed companies with independent management teams and strategies.
Wider Sports News.copy:
US media giant Comcast’s decision to separate its content and broadcasting division from its cable and broadband business is the latest in a series of recent transactions and restructures within the industry designed to reflect changing consumption habits and the disruption caused by streaming.

Wider Sports News.title: 30 years of Opta: How a marketing exercise became the foundation of soccer’s AI future
Wider Sports News.summary: Three decades ago, an upstart management consultancy kickstarted a data revolution in English soccer. Now, Opta is at the forefront of an AI-driven transformation of how the sport is watched, played and understood. The post 30 years of Opta: How a marketing exercise became the foundation of soccer’s AI future appeared first on SportsPro .
Wider Sports News.body:
Data is now so firmly entrenched within soccer that it seems peculiar that there was a time when analytics were treated with suspicion and derision, at least in the UK. Whereas sports like baseball and cricket have long revelled in the minutiae, informing how they are played, watched and understood, the extent of analysis within soccer was limited to a few pockets of innovation around the world and rudimentary metrics.

Now, data is everywhere – from performance and recruitment through to media coverage and sponsorship activations – with more advanced insights changing the way soccer is covered.

Wider Sports News.body:
Hyderabad (Telangana) [India], June 29 (ANI): Actor Mrunal Thakur praised the HSBC Rugby Premier League Season 2 after attending the tournament's final in Hyderabad, where the Hyderabad Heroes defeated the Mumbai Dreamers 41-17 to lift the title.

According to a press release, the Dreamers controlled possession in the early stages but failed to convert it into points as Hyderabad's disciplined defence forced crucial turnovers.

Wider Sports News.title: Hyderabad Heroes crowned HSBC Rugby Premier League season 2 champions
Wider Sports News.summary: Hyderabad Heroes crowned HSBC Rugby Premier League season 2 champions Hyderabad Mail
Wider Sports News.body:
HYDERABAD: Hyderabad Heroes won the HSBC Rugby Premier League Season 2. They beat the Mumbai Dreamers 41-17 in the final at Gachibowli Stadium in Hyderabad on Sunday. Hyderabad Heroes collected the championship trophy and a prize of Rs. 45 lakhs. Mumbai Dreamers secured second place and received Rs. 25 lakhs. Bengaluru Bravehearts finished third and took home Rs. 15 lakhs.

In addition, six men’s teams played 34 matches over 13 days. The league featured 84 players, including 54 international players from 19 countries and 30 Indian players. The tournament drew strong competition and built a reputation as a leading rugby sevens event in the region. This season, the league launched a women’s competition for the first time. Four teams played 14 matches with 48 players. Delhi Redz claimed the title. The new women’s league gave female athletes a professional stage and promoted inclusion.

Wider Sports News.title: HSBC UK confirms sponsorship of major event
Wider Sports News.summary: HSBC UK confirms sponsorship of major event The Scottish Farmer
Wider Sports News.body:
HSBC UK has confirmed its sponsorship of the EDF Congress Chester 2026. The event will focus on ‘new challenges new opportunities’ in the dairy industry. As part of the programme, HSBC UK Agriculture will deliver a practical workshop titled Financing Dairies’ Future.

The session will help farmers and industry stakeholders connect climate and operational risks with actionable investment plans and appropriate funding structures. Steve Dunkley, regional agriculture director (North of England) at HSBC UK, said: "We’re using the workshop to help farmers turn those risks into a practical plan and a finance structure that keeps the business cashflow-safe in a bad year.

Wider Sports News.title: Fox uses World Cup to push IndyCar ratings to 15-year high
Wider Sports News.summary: Road America averaged remarkable 1.8m viewers thanks to starting directly after Spain vs Saudi Arabia game. The post Fox uses World Cup to push IndyCar ratings to 15-year high appeared first on SportsPro .
Wider Sports News.body:
The Fifa World Cup helped IndyCar enjoy its biggest US television audience for a race other than the Indianapolis 500 for 15 years. Fox’s coverage of the 2026 Grand Prix at Road America was scheduled immediately after a World Cup group stage clash between Spain and Saudi Arabia, with two thirds (64 per cent) of soccer viewers sticking around for the race, which averaged 1.8 million.

This was no accident. IndyCar and Fox have developed a concerted strategy designed to maximise the series’ visibility while the World Cup is on North American soil and use live soccer as a lead-in.

Wider Sports News.title: Bisleri Comes Onboard as Official Hydration Partner for HSBC Rugby Premier League
Wider Sports News.summary: Bisleri Comes Onboard as Official Hydration Partner for HSBC Rugby Premier League TheWire.in
Wider Sports News.body:
Bisleri International, India’s leading packaged drinking water company, has announced a three-year association with the HSBC Rugby Premier League (RPL) as the league’s Official Hydration Partner. Through the partnership, Bisleri will support hydration requirements for national and international men’s and women’s players participating in the HSBC Rugby Premier League over the next three seasons, beginning with Season 2.

As Bisleri continues to strengthen its presence across sporting platforms in India, the partnership reinforces the brand’s focus on athlete wellness and deeper consumer engagement through high-impact sporting ecosystems. Launched by GMR Sports along with Rugby India, the HSBC Rugby Premier League has rapidly emerged as a key platform driving the growth of rugby in India, further strengthened this year with the introduction of women’s franchises in Hyderabad. The association will also include digital-first campaigns, on-ground activations, enhanced in-stadium experiences and community-led initiatives aimed at strengthening fan engagement and expanding the league’s reach both on and off the field.

Wider Sports News.title: GSE Worldwide hosts sold-out Laser Wolf during Championship week in Southampton
Wider Sports News.summary: The exclusive evening featured Chef Michael Solomonov, US Ryder Cup captain Jim Furyk and broadcaster Scott Jennings. The post GSE Worldwide hosts sold-out Laser Wolf during Championship week in Southampton appeared first on SportsPro .
Wider Sports News.body:
GSE Worldwide (GSE) hosted a sold-out Laser Wolf pop-up during US Open Championship week in Southampton, bringing together a standing-room-only crowd for an exclusive evening of hospitality, culinary excellence and golf.

Presented by FlyHouse, the one-night-only experience featured a multi-course dinner from five-time James Beard Award-winning Chef Michael Solomonov and his acclaimed restaurant, Laser Wolf, along with a special appearance by US Ryder Cup Captain Jim Furyk. The evening also included a fireside conversation and tournament preview with Furyk moderated by Scott Jennings. All three special guests are represented by GSE.

Wider Sports News.title: The business of Wimbledon: Careful modernisation of tennis tradition protects a unique commercial model
Wider Sports News.summary: Wimbledon's brand preservation over revenue maximisation has made it tennis's most prestigious event. Now it is seeking to modernise its facilities and broadcast coverage to maintain this position. The post The business of Wimbledon: Careful modernisation of tennis tradition protects a unique commercial model appeared first on SportsPro .
Wider Sports News.body:
In the fast-paced, commercially driven world of modern sport, Wimbledon remains steadfastly committed to tradition. The All-England Club (AELTC) isn’t immune from the forces of modernity – far from it. But this focus on heritage and legacy has maintained Wimbledon’s position as the most prestigious tournament in tennis, the anchor of the British sporting summer and an icon of national culture.

An all-white dress code, the meticulously manicured grass courts and the egalitarian perception of the queue are all designed to reinforce this status – and any commercial decision taken by the AELTC must reinforce this status rather than detract from it.

Wider Sports News.body:
As part of the new five-year agreement, Fever will provide a new ticketing platform for fans on F1.com for official general admission, local hospitality, and Paddock Club tickets, with a fresh new look and more sophisticated technology to improve discoverability, purchasing and customer service.

The agreement reflects the shared commitment from Formula One and Fever to develop cutting-edge innovative solutions that continue to enhance the fan experience and the global distribution of official Formula One tickets and hospitality packages.

Wider Sports News.summary: Airline reportedly remains committed to new tournament but is wary of optics amid current geopolitical climate. The post Qatar Airways ‘pauses’ UK£80m rugby Nations Championship title sponsorship appeared first on SportsPro .
Wider Sports News.body:
Qatar Airways’ UK£80 million (US$105.36 million) title sponsorship of rugby union’s Nations Championship has reportedly been paused due to ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

A deal between the airline and the new competition was first touted two years ago, with an eight-year agreement expiring in 2034 understood to have been reached as early as last January.

Wider Sports News.title: Wimbledon and BBC extend to 2033 and agree to refresh coverage
Wider Sports News.summary: Sport's longest running relationship will extend beyond a century with new deal. The post Wimbledon and BBC extend to 2033 and agree to refresh coverage appeared first on SportsPro .
Wider Sports News.body:
Wimbledon has extended its domestic broadcast partnership with the BBC, keeping the tennis Grand Slam on free-to-air (FTA) television until 2033 and extending sports media’s longest relationship beyond a century.

The BBC will celebrate 100 years since its first Wimbledon radio broadcast in 2027 and the tournament remains one of the broadcaster’s most valuable sports properties, with last year’s men’s singles final drawing a peak linear audience of 8.3 million and generating a record 69.3 million online requests.

Rugby.title: Tony Rowe: 'Black Knight Rugby are committed totally to supporting women's rugby'
Rugby.summary: Exeter Chiefs exec Tony Rowe has stated that the team's new owners are 'committed totally' to supporting the club's Premiership Women's Rugby side.
Rugby.body:
Exeter Chiefs exec Tony Rowe has stated that the team’s new owners are ‘committed totally’ to supporting the club’s Premiership Women’s Rugby side. This week Cannae Holdings’ Black Knight Rugby completed a £45m takeover of the Devon club.

R0we – who funded the club’s rise to top tier men’s rugby, two English league titles and a European crown – will continue in his role as CEO for the next two years. Cannae Holdings also operate Premier League side AFC Bournemouth, the NHL’s Vegas Golden Knights, French club FC Lorient and New Zealand’s Auckland FC.

Rugby.title: Champions Cup draw: Saints pitted with Bath as Saracens grouped with 3 winners
Rugby.summary: Saracens have emerged with arguably the toughest draw of any Gallagher PREM club after being placed in a Investec Champions Cup pool featuring three winners for the 2026/27 campaign.
Rugby.body:
Saracens and Exeter Chiefs have landed arguably the toughest Champions Cup draw after being placed in an Investec Champions Cup pool featuring four formers winners for the 2026/27 campaign.

Mark McCall’s side have been drawn in Pool 2 alongside six-time European champions and newly crowned Top 14 winners, Toulouse, two-time winners La Rochelle and 2020 winners Exeter Chiefs, who will be buoyed by the recent takeover by Black Knight. The Lions and Connacht, both quarter-finalists in this season’s United Rugby Championship, complete the group.

Rugby.title: Exeter boss makes extraordinary vow after US takeover
Rugby.summary: Tony Rowe has never been shy about his ambitions for Exeter Chiefs, and he says that he wants to help new owners Black Knight Rugby make them the best club side in the world.
Rugby.body:
Tony Rowe has never been shy about his ambitions for Exeter Chiefs, and he says that he wants to help new owners Black Knight Rugby make them the best club side in the world. Black Knight Rugby completed their £45m takeover of the Gallagher Prem finalists on Tuesday, and Rowe, who has bankrolled the club for 25 years, is staying on for the next two years as chief executive.

After the club were promoted to the top flight of English rugby, Rowe said that he wanted the club to become Premiership and European champions, and people scoffed at the thought.

Rugby.title: Fiji bring former World Cup final referee into camp as veteran gets call
Rugby.summary: Fiji have called up veteran tighthead Mesake Doge to replace the injured Luke Tagi ahead of Saturday's Nations Championship opener against Wales, as the Pacific Islanders also look to sharpen their discipline under the guidance of former Test referee Alain Rolland.
Rugby.body:
Fiji have called up veteran tighthead Mesake Doge to replace the injured Luke Tagi ahead of Saturday’s Nations Championship opener against Wales, as the Pacific Islanders also look to sharpen their discipline under the guidance of former Test referee Alain Rolland.

Tagi, the 20st Bayonne prop, has pulled out for personal reasons, having featured in five matches during the 2023 Rugby World Cup, two wins and three losses, as Fiji were eliminated at the quarter-final stage, and his experience would have been a key factor for a Fijian team that is determined to put in a disciplined performance.

Rugby.title: New US owners can make Chiefs 'best club in the world'
Rugby.summary: Chiefs chairman Tony Rowe says new American investors will maximise the salary cap and invest in women's game.
Rugby.body:
Exeter Chiefs' takeover by American investors can lead to the side becoming "the best club in the world", according to boss Tony Rowe. Cannae Holdings' Black Knight Rugby, run by the same group that owns Premier League football side AFC Bournemouth and NHL side Las Vegas Black Knights, completed their acquisition of the Prem side on Tuesday.

Rowe, who had bankrolled the club for 25 years, will stay on as chief executive and be part of a three-man executive board alongside Cannae's vice-chairman Bill Foley and chief executive Ryan Caswell.

Rugby.title: Salvin leaves Exeter for Wales forwards coach job
Rugby.summary: Wales boost backroom team before WXV by bringing in forwards coach Steve Salvin on a full-time basis.
Rugby.body:
Steve Salvin has left his role as Exeter boss to become full-time forwards coach with Wales. Salvin was part of Sean Lynn's management team for the Six Nations on an interim basis but has now left the Chiefs after the Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) season.

He will link up with Wales when they return for pre-season on Monday, 3 August as they start preparations for the WXV. "The chance to work at Test match level during the recent W6N [Women's Six Nations] campaign was an opportunity that I relished and to get to do this on a full-time basis is exciting," said Salvin.

Rugby.title: Salvin departs Exeter after one season as head coach for full-time Test rugby role
Rugby.summary: Steve Salvin has left Exeter Chiefs after one season as head coach to take on a full-time role as Wales' forwards coach.
Rugby.body:
Steve Salvin has left Exeter Chiefs after one season as head coach to take on a full-time role as Wales’ forwards coach. In his sole season as head coach of the Devon club, Salvin took Chiefs back to knockout rugby at the first time of asking. Exeter were beaten 40-38 by eventual Premiership Women’s Rugby champions Saracens in the semi-final.

Salvin was seconded to Wales as their forwards coach on a part-time basis for the 2026 Guinness Women’s Six Nations. Now the former Rotherham Titans, Leeds Carnegie and Worcester Warriors coach will now help Wales prepare for their WXV Global Series fixtures in September and August.

Rugby.title: England can cope with altitude
Rugby.summary: England will be ideally prepared for the unique challenge of playing at altitude against world champions South Africa on Saturday, says coach Sir Kevin Sinfield.
Rugby.body:
England will be ideally prepared for the unique challenge of playing at altitude against world champions South Africa on Saturday, says coach Sir Kevin Sinfield. The iconic Ellis Park in Johannesburg hosts England's Nations Championship opener against the Springboks, a venue nearly 6,000ft above sea level and a place where the visitors have not won since 1972.

But having flown out to South Africa 10 days in advance of the game, the players will have had the ideal amount of time to adapt to the conditions, according to Sinfield. "The timescale we allowed here was supposed to be the optimal time for the average person to have adapted and adjusted to the altitude," he told the Rugby Union Weekly podcast.

Rugby.title: 'It feels like the right time to step away': 30 cap Red Roses lock retires
Rugby.summary: England Red Roses lock Cath O'Donnell has announced her retirement from rugby at the age of 30.
Rugby.body:
England Red Roses lock Cath O’Donnell has announced her retirement from rugby at the age of 30. The 30-cap international, who represented Loughborough Lightning in Premiership Women’s Rugby, has not played a game in two years. Her final match for the East Midlanders came against Saracens at the conclusion of the 2023/24 season.

O’Donnell burst onto the scene nine years ago while still a student at the University of Liverpool. During her time on Merseyside the Cumbria native also turned out for top flight club Waterloo where she earned England Under-20 caps.

Rugby.title: Scarlets announce £2.1m loss in 'challenging' time
Rugby.summary: Scarlets made a loss of £2.1m during the 2024-2025 season, according to the club's latest accounts.
Rugby.body:
Scarlets made a loss of £2.1m during the 2024-2025 season, according to the club's latest accounts. It is a third year in a row the Welsh region have posted a loss of more than £2m.

The financial results cover the period to June 2025, with the loss down from £2.6m from the previous 12 months. Turnover dropped from £10.2m compared to £11.3m with gate receipts, sponsorship and money received from the Welsh Rugby Union (WRU) all down.

Rugby.title: England football legend the first to congratulate Sir Kevin Sinfield
Rugby.summary: Sir Kevin Sinfield has revealed that Alan Shearer was the first to offer congratulations as part of an “overwhelming” response to his knighthood that has made him appreciate his return to England duties.
Rugby.body:
Sir Kevin Sinfield has revealed that Alan Shearer was the first to offer congratulations as part of an “overwhelming” response to his knighthood that has made him appreciate his return to England duties.

Sinfield was recently honoured for services to rugby and for his fundraising for Motor Neurone Disease, which began after his best friend and former Leeds Rhinos team-mate Rob Burrow was diagnosed with the neurological condition in 2019.

Rugby.title: Will Marshall mellow for Galthie's strengthened France?
Rugby.summary: Les Bleus came in for criticism when arrived in New Zealand with a fallow side a year ago but this French team is a different proposition
Rugby.body:
France play New Zealand on Saturday and so far it’s all quiet on the Marshall front. Perhaps France’s fiercest foe will launch a new offensive between now and Saturday or maybe Justin Marshall is satisfied with the squad selected by Fabien Galthie for the opening match of the Nations Championship.

Twelve months ago Marshall went wild over the under-strength French squad that turned up in New Zealand for a three-Test series; and the former All Black scrum-half was at it again last November, battering the Bleus once more.

Rugby.title: Nations Championship: 'The data shows the north has finally caught up with the south'
Rugby.summary: A Six Nations statistical deep dive suggests the European nations are no longer trailing their Rugby Championship counterparts.
Rugby.body:
While some events leave the soul untouched, others leave a permanent mark – maybe even a little scar tissue. We never know when the turning points will arrive in advance, but only get to know them afterwards.

Three months after the tournament finished with a remarkable 13-try extravaganza, a last-gasp 48-46 victory by France over England, the reverberations of the 2026 Six Nations are still being felt as the inaugural Nations Championship looms large. It is one of those landmark events which will leave a permanent stamp on the game.

Rugby.title: Losing to Henry Pollock would be unbearable
Rugby.summary: There is no brandy and Coke mix strong enough for what would happen if Henry Pollock wins at Ellis Park.
Rugby.body:
There is no brandy and Coke mix strong enough for what would happen if Henry Pollock wins at Ellis Park. Not England. Not Steve Borthwick. Not Marcus Smith gliding into space or Ben Earl running in on the angle. Henry Pollock. The blond hair, the grin, the bounce, the camera-aware strut, the whole infuriating package. If England triumph in the City of Gold for the first time since 1972, that would sting. Anyone but England, and all that.

But knowing that Pollock had a hand in it, the national headloss might be visible from space. South African rugby has had villains before. Real ones. Manufactured ones. English ones. Martin Johnson was too hard to disrespect. Jonny Wilkinson was too good to ignore. Owen Farrell was, well, Owen Farrell.

Rugby.title: Tyler Offiah: The budding actor and legend's son who wants to 'complete rugby'
Rugby.summary: Bearing an iconic surname, the England U20s wing has huge ambitions, but is refreshingly candid about his fluctuating form.
Rugby.body:
At eight years old, blinking back tears, Tyler Offiah cast an envious glance at his new holiday mates cavorting around the hotel complex. He couldn’t understand why he was on the grass, with his father and a rugby ball, instead of joining the fun. He couldn’t fathom the dedication and standards being drilled into him even at such a tender age. He didn’t know these childhood sessions would propel his rise to a professional career, or help his younger brother Phoenix earn a place in the Tottenham Hotspur football academy.

He did know his dad was a legend. Martin ‘Chariots’ Offiah is one of the finest players in rugby league history, his legacy enshrined in a bronze statue outside Wembley. “Since I was two, I’ve been chucking a ball about and my dad was one of my coaches,” Offiah says. “I’ve always associated him with rugby.

Rugby.title: Bok Women eye forward domination and World Rugby rankings rise in 2026
Rugby.summary: The Springbok Women want to dominate the USA Women's Eagles at Ellis Park this Saturday in the first fixture of a two-Test series.
Rugby.body:
The Springbok Women want to dominate the USA Women’s Eagles at Ellis Park this Saturday in the first fixture of a two-Test series. Ahead of the Springbok Men’s clash with England in their Nations Championship opener, Swys de Bruin’s team take on an Eagles side they have only ever met six times in the past. To date the USA have won five of those matches.

It will be the first time that the Springbok Women have played a home Test since their Women’s Rugby World Cup 2025 campaign, in which De Bruin’s team reached the quarter-final stage of the tournament for the first time.

Rugby.title: Wales rugby quiz: Test your summer tour knowledge
Rugby.summary: Test yourself with our quiz on Wales' men's past five summer tours.
Rugby.body:
Wales begin the Nations Championship when they face Fiji in Cardiff on Saturday, before trips to Argentina and South Africa. The new tournament means a change from traditional tours at this time of year, but how well do you remember Wales' recent summer matches?

Test yourself with our quiz on the past five summers of rugby for the men's national team. After more quizzes? Go to our dedicated Football Quizzes and Sports Quizzes pages and sign up for notifications to get the latest quizzes sent straight to your device.

Rugby.title: Rugby Union Weekly
Rugby.summary: Chris and Paul Grayson have made it to South Africa and catch up with Sir Kevin Sinfield.
Rugby.body: Chris and Paul Grayson are in Johannesburg ahead of England’s huge Nations Championship opener against South Africa. They are joined by the sport’s newest knight, Sir Kevin Sinfield, to talk about being awarded the knighthood and his determination to continue raising awareness for MND. Sinfield discusses England’s approach this weekend at Ellis Park and how they are dealing with the unique challenges of playing in the spiritual home of Springbok rugby.

Rugby.title: Where's the power? And what to do with Sua'ali'i? The key questions facing the Wallabies
Rugby.summary: As he prepares to hand the coaching reins to Les Kiss, Australia coach Joe Schmidt will look to provide answers to several selection quandaries.
Rugby.body:
The first Wallabies squad of the year brings new excitement, with Australian fans trying to work out which parts will go where, as players switch from Super Rugby to international rugby.

It’s also the beginning of the end of the Joe Schmidt era. Schmidt will take charge for these first three Nations Championship Tests, with a concerted, gradual increase in Les Kiss’ presence ahead of the completed takeover for the Japan series and the not-Rugby Championship Tests in August and September.

Rugby.title: 2025/26 Premiership Women's Rugby | RugbyPass' Team of the Season
Rugby.summary: Sunday afternoon saw Saracens pick up their fourth Premiership Women's Rugby title in style with a 52-14 win over Trailfinders Women in the Final.
Rugby.body:
Sunday afternoon saw Saracens pick up their fourth Premiership Women’s Rugby title in style with a 52-14 win over Trailfinders Women in the Final. The fixture at the Twickenham Stoop was a last chance for the league’s best players to strut their stuff this season. This season we have been treated to some sensational solo performances across the eighth campaign in the PWR era.

As we now head into the offseason there is just enough time to see which players shone the most. So, here is RugbyPass’ 2025/26 PWR Team of the Season… Emma Sing’s strong domestic performances do not come as a shock at this stage. In 2025/26 the 25-year-old continued to be a major influence for Gloucester Hartpury as she picked up a gaggle of Player of the Match awards, 114 points and 635 metres from 113 carries. The continuation of her strong domestic form was not enough to take Gloucester Hartpury to a fourth PWR title in as many years, but it was memorable nonetheless.

Rugby.title: ITV pauses scrum ad slots for July Tests
Rugby.summary: In-play, in-picture adverts will not be part of ITV's coverage of the Nations Championship in July, but the 20-second slots are set to return in November.
Rugby.body:
In-play, in-picture adverts will not be part of ITV's coverage of the Nations Championship in July, but the 20-second slots, shown before a scrum, are set to return for the November Tests and next year's Six Nations.

The abundance of opportunities in ITV's schedule in July, with an expanded 48-team football World Cup dominating the airwaves, means advertisers' spending has been directed elsewhere.

Rugby.title: Nigel Davies lands permanent Scarlets role after conducting review
Rugby.summary: Nigel Davies has been appointed Scarlets' permanent director of rugby on a two-year deal after overseeing the club on an interim basis since January.
Rugby.body:
Nigel Davies has been appointed Scarlets’ permanent director of rugby on a two-year deal after overseeing the club on an interim basis since January. The former Scarlets player, captain and head coach will now lead the region into the 2026/27 season after conducting a wide-ranging review of the rugby department over the past five months.

Davies has already overseen a significant rebuild ahead of the new campaign, with Wales prop Corey Domachowski, Leinster hooker John McKee, Crusaders forward Cullen Grace, Stormers lock Alex Groves, Bath second-row Harvey Cuckson and Canterbury full-back Isaac Murray-MacGregor all arriving at Parc y Scarlets this summer.

Rugby.title: Rassie Erasmus pipped by fierce rival in Nations Championship win percentages
Rugby.summary: While Dave Rennie is just about to start his tenure as head coach of the All Blacks, Rassie Erasmus will draw level with Jake White's record of 54 Tests in charge of the Springboks this Saturday.
Rugby.body:
While Dave Rennie is just about to start his tenure as head coach of the All Blacks, Rassie Erasmus is eight years in and will draw level with Jake White’s record of 54 Tests in charge of the Springboks, when he leads South Africa into battle against England at Ellis Park this Saturday.

Two Rugby World Cup wins, a Lions series victory, three Rugby Championship titles and a ranking of number one in the world makes Erasmus the standout coach amongst the current crop.

Rugby.title: Scarlets confirm Davies as boss on two-year deal
Rugby.summary: Scarlets will be led by former Wales centre Nigel Davies for the next two seasons.
Rugby.body:
Scarlets have appointed Nigel Davies as director of rugby for the next two seasons. The former Wales centre returned to Llanelli on an interim basis in January to work above head coach Dwayne Peel after a poor first half of the season.

Scarlets were bottom of the United Rugby Championship (URC) but climbed above Zebre and Dragons. After making seven signings and bolstering the backroom staff, Davies has been confirmed as boss for the next two campaigns.

Rugby.title: Heyes signs new four-year Leicester Tigers deal
Rugby.summary: Leicester Tigers' England prop Joe Heyes signs a new four-year contract with the Prem club.
Rugby.body:
Leicester Tigers' England prop Joe Heyes has signed a new four-year contract with the Prem club. The 27-year-old has featured more than 180 times across all competitions for Tigers' senior side since he emerged from the club's academy in 2018.

Heyes made his England debut in 2021 and, within a year of that milestone international moment, he helped Tigers win the 2022 Premiership title. He has gone on to earn a total of 22 caps and has been included in the England squad for this summer's Nations Championship competition, which starts against South Africa on Saturday.

Rugby.title: England Player of the Year Joe Heyes signs long-term Leicester deal
Rugby.summary: Heyes, 27, has enjoyed a stellar season for club and country, and was voted England Rugby Player of the Year at the recent Rugby Awards do in London.
Rugby.body:
England tight-head Joe Heyes has committed to playing for his boyhood club Leicester into his 30s after signing a new four-year deal. Heyes, 27, has enjoyed a stellar season for club and country, and was voted England Rugby Player of the Year at the recent Rugby Awards do in London.

Having been with the club since he was 16, Heyes made his senior debut in 2018 and is the most experienced member of the squad, with close to 200 appearances, now that Harry Wells has moved on.

Rugby.title: Experienced Ireland players sent warning by staff over next generation
Rugby.summary: Ireland assistant coach Simon Easterby feels the players must be ready to face an Australia side looking to “lay down a bit of a marker” for the World Cup when they kick off their Nations Championship campaign in Sydney.
Rugby.body:
Ireland assistant coach Simon Easterby feels the players must be ready to face an Australia side looking to “lay down a bit of a marker” for the World Cup when they kick off their Nations Championship campaign in Sydney.

Andy Farrell’s 36-strong squad – which has three uncapped players, with Dan Sheehan set to stand in as captain – face the Wallabies on Saturday looking to push on again following a promising Guinness Six Nations campaign where they finished second behind France.

Rugby.title: Scotland admit there's 'definitely an edge' ahead of Argentina clash
Rugby.summary: Pieter de Villiers detects an extra edge around the Scotland camp as they gear up for their first match of the inaugural Nations Championship away to Argentina.
Rugby.body:
Pieter de Villiers detects an extra edge around the Scotland camp as they gear up for their first match of the inaugural Nations Championship away to Argentina. The Scots face a formidable start to the new biennial tournament, pitting southern hemisphere sides against their northern hemisphere counterparts as they meet the Pumas in Cordoba on Saturday followed by South Africa in Pretoria a week later.

At a time when relatively low-key summer tours usually take place, Scotland scrum coach De Villiers is relishing the extra competitive element to the upcoming fixtures. “It’s a different kind of competition, a different kind of tour with this new Nations Cup arriving, immediately going into a game against number five (in the world),” he said. “Prepping after that for a game against number one, both in their own countries.

Rugby.title: Easterby expects 'marker' from Australia in Sydney
Rugby.summary: Ireland coach Simon Easterby says he expects Australia to try and "impose themselves" in Saturday's Nations Championship opener in Sydney.
Rugby.body:
Ireland coach Simon Easterby says he expects Australia to try and "impose themselves" in Saturday's Nations Championship opener in Sydney (11:00 BST). Joe Schmidt's Wallabies host Ireland in the first of three summer matches in the new competition, before Andy Farrell's side take on Japan and New Zealand.

Farrell led the British and Irish Lions' successful tour of Australia last year and Ireland ran out 46-19 winners when the sides met in Dublin in November. Easterby, who said all 36 players were in training in Sydney, said it was "exciting" to face Australia, who will host the Rugby World Cup next year.

Rugby.title: Exeter's US takeover completed as Black Knight take charge
Rugby.summary: The owners of Premier League club Bournemouth complete their takeover of Prem Rugby side Exeter Chiefs.
Rugby.body:
The owners of Premier League club Bournemouth have completed their takeover of Prem Rugby side Exeter Chiefs. Cannae Holdings' Black Knight Rugby are the first US-based group to take control of a top-flight side in English rugby.

BBC Sport understands the deal for this month's beaten Prem finalists, which includes their women's side, is worth in the region of £25m. Exeter's members, who had owned the two-time Prem champions and 2020 European Champions Cup winners, voted last month to to approve the plans.

Rugby.title: Black Knight complete Exeter takeover to become PREM's first US investors
Rugby.summary: Exeter Chiefs have confirmed that they have been taken over by Black Knight Rugby in a £45 million deal, ending Tony Rowe’s 25 years of funding the club.
Rugby.body:
Exeter Chiefs have confirmed that they have been taken over by Black Knight Rugby in a £45 million deal, ending Tony Rowe’s 25 years of funding the club. The Chiefs will now be a new subsidiary of Cannae Holdings, Inc after Black Knight Rugby purchased 100% of the shares of Exeter Rugby Group Ltd following a vote by Exeter’s members.

It has been confirmed that Rowe, who helped the Exeter Chiefs survive a £25 million debt accrued following the COVID-19 pandemic, will continue as CEO for the next two years alongside AFC Bournemouth owner Bill Foley and Cannae Holdings CEO Ryan Caswell.

Rugby.title: Ex-England U20 star among two uncapped players in 33-man Wales squad
Rugby.summary: Steve Tandy has confirmed his 33-man Wales squad for the opening block of Nations Championship fixtures, with Exeter Chiefs back-row Kane James and Ospreys tighthead Ben Warren the only uncapped players included.
Rugby.body:
Steve Tandy has confirmed his 33-man Wales squad for the opening block of Nations Championship fixtures, with Exeter Chiefs back-row Kane James and Ospreys tighthead Ben Warren the only uncapped players included.

Haverfordwest-born James, who represented England U20 before switching allegiance to Wales, is rewarded after impressing in his first full season with Exeter, while Warren earns his first senior call-up after breaking into the Ospreys front-row.

Rugby.title: Exeter Chiefs left counting the cost of their Gallagher Prem Final defeat
Rugby.summary: Exeter Chiefs boss Rob Baxter expects captain Dafydd Jenkins to be fit for the start of next season after announcing that a shoulder injury will force him to miss Wales’ inaugural Nations Championship campaign.
Rugby.body:
Exeter Chiefs boss Rob Baxter expects captain Dafydd Jenkins to be fit for the start of next season after announcing that a shoulder injury will force him to miss Wales’ inaugural Nations Championship campaign.

It has been revealed that the Chiefs lock, who scored a try and was sin-binned, sustained a shoulder injury during the Gallagher PREM Final against Northampton Saints at Twickenham 10 days ago, when he was replaced by Ross Vintcent.

Rugby.title: Five must-watch Nations Cup matches as an All Black dons new colours
Rugby.summary: The inaugural Nations Cup kicks off this Saturday with 12 teams involved in what is expected to be an exhilarating and dramatic race to the top.
Rugby.body:
The inaugural Nations Cup kicks off this Saturday with 12 teams involved in what is expected to be an exhilarating and dramatic race to the top. But with three action-packed weekends on our doorstep, what games deserve the main spotlight? Between longtime rivalries and fierce showdowns, find out what our picks are!

Tonga vs Zimbabwe (July 4, 11.15 PM GMT) Over the next 14 months, Tonga and Zimbabwe will be facing each other twice, as not only are they due to clash this weekend, but they will also be entangled in pool F of the upcoming Rugby World Cup.

Rugby.title: Wales lose Exeter lock Jenkins for summer Tests
Rugby.summary: Wales trim their squad for the Nations Championship after losing Exeter lock Dafydd Jenkins to injury.
Rugby.body:
Wales lock Dafydd Jenkins has been ruled out of the summer Nations Championship Tests because of a shoulder injury suffered with Exeter. The 23-year-old is not in Steve Tandy's 33-strong squad for the fixtures against Fiji, Argentina and South Africa.

Jenkins, who started all five Six Nations fixtures, suffered a blow in the Chiefs' Prem final defeat by Northampton and needs surgery. There are two uncapped players in Tandy's cut-down squad. They are Ospreys tight-head prop Ben Warren and Exeter back-row forward Kane James.

Rugby.title: Ex-Ireland internationals Briggs and Muldoon land provincial women's rugby lead roles
Rugby.summary: Irish Rugby have appointed four provincial women's rugby leadership roles as the union aims to continue the growth of women's rugby in the country.
Rugby.body:
Irish Rugby have appointed four provincial women’s rugby leadership roles as the union aims to continue the growth of women’s rugby in the country. Former Ireland fly-half and Ireland Under-21 coach Niamh Briggs is among the appointments and has been named Women’s Pathway Manager with her home province, Munster.

Larissa Muldoon – who won 48 caps for Ireland – has been named Connacht’s women’s rugby lead, Ulster have named Neill Alcorn as their provincial pathway manager and Derek Maybury will be Leinster’s provincial lead.

Larissa Muldoon – who won 48 caps for Ireland – has been named Connacht’s women’s rugby lead, Ulster have named Neill Alcorn as their provincial pathway manager and Derek Maybury will be Leinster’s provincial lead.

Rugby.title: England's Seb Atkinson downplays South Africa's legendary fear factor
Rugby.summary: De Allende has been a mainstay of South Africa’s successive World Cup triumphs.
Rugby.body:
Seb Atkinson is ready to leave his mark on a player who has influenced his own game when he collides with Damian de Allende in England’s clash with South Africa on Saturday. Atkinson made his Gallagher Prem debut against Manu Tuilagi and then faced Springbok giant Andre Esterhuizen in his first league start, but at Ellis Park awaits arguably a tougher assignment than either of those midfield juggernauts.

De Allende has been a mainstay of South Africa’s successive World Cup triumphs and with 97 caps the 6’2”, 17 stone inside centre has the experience to match his physical prowess.

Rugby.title: Halfpenny joins Wales coach team for summer Tests
Rugby.summary: Legendary former full-back Leigh Halfpenny will work as Wales' interim kicking coach for the Nations Championship.
Rugby.body:
Leigh Halfpenny has joined the Wales management team for the Nations Championship as kicking coach. The former full-back retired at the end of the 2025-26 season and has linked up with Steve Tandy's squad for summer Tests against Fiji, Argentina and South Africa.

Halfpenny worked with Wales last summer on their two-Test tour of Japan as skills coach with responsibility for kicking. "I thoroughly enjoyed my experience in Japan last year and I'm looking forward to getting to work with the group and continuing my development as a coach over the next few weeks," said the 37-year-old.

Rugby.title: North v South
Rugby.summary: The new Nations Championship pits the northern hemisphere against the south. Traditionally there would only be one winner in such a contest. Perhaps things are different now...
Rugby.body:
The scoreline is emphatic: 9-1 and counting. When it comes to Rugby World Cups, nine of the 10 have been lifted by southern hemisphere hands. Only Jonny Wilkinson's extra-time drop-goal for England in the 2003 final stands between the south and a clean sweep of global glory.

So, a new Test competition pitting one half of the globe against the other will be long-haul, but intrigue-short. Won't it? The Nations Championship, which begins this weekend, might be a concept arriving at the perfect time.

Rugby.title: Springbok hope high-intensity training can prepare squad for USA Tests
Rugby.summary: The Springbok Women are hoping that their high-intensity training can make up for a lack of game time in 2026.
Rugby.body:
The Springbok Women are hoping that their high-intensity training can make up for a lack of game time in 2026. This calendar year Swys de Bruin’s team have successfully defended their Rugby Africa Women’s Cup title in Kenya, but only selected nine players from their Women’s Rugby World Cup 2025 squad.

For their two-Test series against the USA Women’s Eagles, De Bruin has named 22 players from last Autumn’s Women’s Rugby World Cup as they prepare to take on a team that have had game-time against Canada, New Zealand and Australia this year in Pacific Four Series competition.

Rugby.title: 'It's a bad injury': Elrigh Louw called up as Springbok injury crisis worsens
Rugby.summary: Bulls back-row Elrigh Louw has been called into the South Africa squad following a hamstring injury to second-row Riley Norton which has ruled him out for 10 weeks.
Rugby.body:
Bulls back-row Elrigh Louw has been called into the South Africa squad following a hamstring injury to second-row Riley Norton which has ruled him out for 10 weeks. South Africa U20 captain Norton is the latest lock to pick up an injury for the Boks, so much so that Rassie Erasmus was unable to name any second-row cover on the bench against England on Saturday for the first match of the Nations Championship.

After naming his squad, Erasmus revealed that 20-year-old Norton was in line to earn his first South Africa cap against Steve Borthwick’s side, before joining the U20 outfit for the remainder of the World Rugby Junior World Championship.

Rugby.title: Junior Wallabies hand nephew of World Cup-winning captain second debut chance
Rugby.summary: Unless he suffers another late mishap, Tom Farr-Jones will make his Junior World Championship debut for Australia against Fiji in Kutaisi this Thursday.
Rugby.body:
Unless he suffers another late mishap, Tom Farr-Jones will make his Junior World Championship debut for Australia against Fiji in Kutaisi this Thursday. The Waratahs winger and nephew of 1991 World Cup-winning captain, Nick Farr-Jones, pulled out of Australia’s opening match – the record 90-22 win over Spain – at the eleventh hour due to illness.

It was particularly galling for Farr-Jones as he’d recovered from finger surgery just in time to make the Junior Wallabies’ squad for Georgia. But now the 20-year-old has another chance to show what he can do as one of 10 changes to the team that racked up Australia’s highest-ever score at the Junior World Championship.

Rugby.title: EPCR announce significant rule changes to Champions Cup and Challenge Cup
Rugby.summary: European Professional Club Rugby (EPCR) have announced a couple of major changes to the rules of the Investec Champions Cup and Challenge Cup, which will come into effect from next season.
Rugby.body:
European Professional Club Rugby (EPCR) have announced a couple of major changes to the rules of the Investec Champions Cup and Challenge Cup, which will come into effect from next season.

Following a plethora of blowout scorelines in the 2025/26 season, EPCR bosses have decided to follow the example of the Top 14 and award try bonus points to the winning tean scoring three or more tries than their opponents, rather than the current system which rewards teams, whether they win or lose, if they cross the whitewash on four or more occasions.

Rugby.title: Bonus-point rule and last-16 changes for Champions Cup
Rugby.summary: Try bonus points in next season's Champions Cup pool stage will be awarded only to teams who score at least three more times than their opponents.
Rugby.body:
Try-scoring bonus points in next season's Champions Cup pool stage will be awarded only to teams who cross at least three more times than their opponents – rather than for simply scoring four or more tries.

The concept, which is used in France's Top 14, the southern hemisphere's Super Rugby and, from next season, will be implemented in England's second-tier Champ, aims to keep matches competitive for longer.

Rugby.title: Neil Barnes labels box kick trend 'overboard', France narrative 'rubbish'
Rugby.summary: All Blacks lead assistant coach Neil Barnes isn't convinced the volume of box kicks in the international game is fully warranted, implying the All Blacks will be less inclined to keep pace with the attacking trend.
Rugby.body:
All Blacks lead assistant coach Neil Barnes isn’t convinced the volume of box kicks in the international game is fully warranted, implying the All Blacks will be less inclined to keep pace with the attacking trend.

New Zealand’s Nations Championship squad came together for the first time last Wednesday, giving Barnes his first chance to work hands-on with his players and begin building the game plan developed since his March 24 appointment.

Rugby.title: ‘Big climb for any team’: Tom Wright on the Wallabies’ Ireland challenge
Rugby.summary: It’s been a fair while since the Wallabies last tasted victory in this matchup, with the Irish winning the last five meetings in a row.
Rugby.body:
The Wallabies are under no illusions about the almighty rugby powerhouse threat that awaits them this weekend, but fullback Tom Wright is “chomping at the bit” as the men in gold look to snap a losing streak against world number three Ireland.

It’s been a fair while since the Wallabies last tasted victory in this matchup, with the Irish winning the last five meetings in a row. That streak dates back to 2018, with the Wallabies last defeating the Six Nations juggernaut 18-9 in Brisbane on June 9.

Rugby.title: There's only one selection that truly matters for the All Blacks this July
Rugby.summary: The All Blacks are relevant again. People will watch, and people will get back on the bandwagon, having kind of given up in disgust of late.
Rugby.body:
I don’t need to see the All Blacks win on Saturday. And, beyond Ruben Love at first five-eighth, I’m not hugely bothered about who plays. I can already see that this team is building, it’s already apparent that they have a plan and it’s already clearly obvious that new coach Dave Rennie is in charge.

I’d expect the All Blacks to beat France in Christchurch. I’ll go further and say I definitely want them to win as well. I haven’t felt that too often in recent seasons. I was quite happy to see Ian Foster and Scott Robertson’s teams lose because I wanted change and didn’t particularly like the players.

Rugby.title: New Zealand U20 make three changes for Scotland
Rugby.summary: New Zealand U20 have welcomed back No.8 Patrick Mauga for round 2 of the Junior World Championship, which will see them face Scotland U20.
Rugby.body:
New Zealand U20 have welcomed back No.8 Patrick Mauga for round 2 of the Junior World Championship, which will see them face Scotland U20. The team won an opening-round match against Japan that was cut short due to nearby lightning strikes, with the scoreline of 38-21 when the game was abandoned after 66 minutes submitted as the final result.

The Baby Blacks’ opponents also scored 38 points in their opening-round win over Italy, conceding 32 points to give New Zealand the top seed in the pool one third of the way through the pool stages.

Rugby.title: Tom Hooper says he has returned to Wallabies 'more of an athlete'
Rugby.summary: Tom Hooper is back on Australian shores and enjoying the "familiar accents", but he also recognises a lot has changed since he was home last and prepping for a Wallabies campaign.
Rugby.body:
Tom Hooper is back on Australian shores and enjoying the “familiar accents”, but he also recognises a lot has changed since he was home last and prepping for a Wallabies campaign.

The versatile forward wrapped up his first season with Exeter Chiefs just last week with a final loss to Northampton Saints, but wasted no time in flying back to Australia to prepare for the Wallabies’ July Nations Championship Tests, fronting media in Sydney just seven days out from an opening Test against Ireland.

Rugby.title: Bench injured Dupont in November? How can France solve their play-making paradox?
Rugby.summary: Fabien Galthie must find a blend between Top 14 winners Toulouse and European champions Bordeaux-Begles to conquer the Springboks this year.
Rugby.body:
It’s a neat trick from the therapist’s couch: when you catch yourself judging someone else, try seeing ways in which you are alike. Do it honestly, and you may even find the common ground of a shared personality feature, a vraisemblance. And hey presto! The judgement vanishes.

‘Both-and’ thinking is far more nutritious chicken soup for the soul than ‘either-or’. It is the same in French rugby at the moment. After their 28-20 victory over Montpellier Hérault at the weekend, Stade Toulousain unquestionably established itself as the best club side in France. Les Rouges-et-Noirs have won the past four Boucliers de Brennus and six of the past seven dating back to pre-Covid days. No argument there.

Rugby.title: Rugby Union Weekly
Rugby.summary: Danny, Ashy & Chris preview the Nations Championship and England's showdown at Ellis Park
Rugby.body: The inaugural Nation's Championship is here, but are fans actually excited about it? Do they even understand the format? Chris, Danny and Ashy dig into the new competition, the logistical nightmare facing travelling supporters and the huge challenge for players playing back-to-back matches in different continents. Rassie Erasmus has already named his team to face England this weekend, does this help with Steve Borthwick's selection dilemmas? Will Henry Pollock be unleashed in Johannesburg? Is it Ben Spencer or Alex Mitchell at nine? Plus, we discuss the three things England need to do if they are to beat the Boks. Ashy shares his memories of playing South Africa at Ellis Park and Danny explains what it’s like to play at altitude and how it effects players. And after Jack Willis captained Toulouse to a record-extending 25th Top 14 title, we debate whether he could ever be lured back to England and what the RFU could do to make it happen.

Rugby.title: 'Your brain picks up there's less oxygen... it hits you from the start'
Rugby.summary: The sign at the Loftus Versfeld tunnel mouth has stood there for years, but as rugby evolves, does altitude still offer a great advantage?
Rugby.body:
As players emerge from the tunnel at Loftus Versfeld, there is a sign waiting for them overhead: “Altitude. 1350m. It matters.” For decades, the Highveld has been treated as rugby’s invisible extra man, a place where visiting sides leave talking about burning lungs, heavy legs and games that slipped away once oxygen debt caught up with them.

Recently, though, that mythology has taken a few blows. Glasgow Warriors won the 2024 URC final at Loftus with a second-half resurgence. A year later, Northampton Saints became the first away side to win at the ground in the Champions Cup. In Johannesburg, the Wallabies overturned a 22-point deficit to stun the Springboks. These results have complicated the old certainty that a trip to Pretoria or Johannesburg is decided as much by geography as rugby.

Rugby.title: Ben Earl: England ‘focusing on ourselves’, not South Africa players’ reputations
Rugby.summary: Ben Earl insists England are focused on winning with style rather than on South Africa’s reputations when the rivals clash at Ellis Park on Saturday.
Rugby.body:
Ben Earl insists England are focused on winning with style rather than on South Africa’s reputations when the rivals clash at Ellis Park on Saturday. England are looking to end a run of four Test defeats that registered their worst ever Six Nations performance when they take on Siya Kolisi’s back-to-back world champions.

A courageous performance against France in the climax to the tournament has provided a blueprint for how next month’s Nations Championship matches should be approached, but Earl has vowed they will not lose sight of the only objective that really matters.

Rugby.title: Belgium international Bruno Vliegen severely burned in accident
Rugby.summary: Belgian international Bruno Vliegen has suffered a serious accident that has left him with burns covering 37 per cent of his body, and the Belgian community has launched an appeal to help cover the costs of his medical treatment.
Rugby.body:
Belgian international Bruno Vliegen has suffered a serious accident that has left him with burns covering 37 per cent of his body, and the Belgian community has launched an appeal to help cover the costs of his medical treatment.

According to Nicolas Gigot, a close friend of Vliegen who shared an update on the Leetchi fundraising platform, he is currently hospitalised at the Grand Hôpital de Charleroi and receiving treatment for severe burns covering 37 per cent of his body.

Rugby.title: Rassie Erasmus doubles down on Henry Pollock verdict
Rugby.summary: South Africa boss Rassie Erasmus views Henry Pollock as England’s dangerman heading into Saturday’s Ellis Park collision.
Rugby.body:
South Africa boss Rassie Erasmus views Henry Pollock as England’s dangerman heading into Saturday’s Ellis Park collision. The back-to-back world champions have named a formidable team for the Nations Championship opener that is led by Siya Kolisi and features a host of stars, including Eben Etzebeth, Pieter-Steph du Toit and Damian de Allende.

England showman Pollock impressed in the climax to Northampton’s Gallagher Prem title march by making hard yards at number eight and he looks set to continue with his high-impact bench role in the first of three Tests next month.

Rugby.title: 'It's made me feel loved': Marlie Packer's perfect Saracens sendoff
Rugby.summary: Marlie Packer felt the love ahead of her last-ever match for Saracens in their Premiership Women's Rugby Final victory against Trailfinders Women.
Rugby.body:
Marlie Packer felt the love ahead of her last-ever match for Saracens in their Premiership Women’s Rugby Final victory against Trailfinders Women. In June it was announced that the 36-year-old England international was leaving StoneX Stadium for pastures new with their capital city rivals, Harlequins, after nine years donning black and red.

As sendoffs go, Packer’s was perfect. As Saracens claimed their fourth English league title of the PWR [previously Premier15s] era the two-time Women’s Rugby World Cup winner crossed the whitewash twice and even received a yellow card at the Twickenham Stoop as the North London side racked up a record 52-14 win.

Rugby.title: Delay as WRU to reveal plan to cut team in 'coming weeks'
Rugby.summary: The Welsh Rugby Union delays revealing the details of how it plans to cut a professional men's side by 2028.
Rugby.body:
The Welsh Rugby Union has delayed revealing details of how they plan to reduce a professional side. In April, the WRU confirmed that proposals to go down to three men's professional teams would not happen until 2028 after the deal with Ospreys owners Y11 Sport & Media to buy Cardiff from Welsh rugby's governing body fell through.

The WRU said it was pushing on with controversial plans to cut from four to three regions and that it would provide details of how it would be achieved by the end of June, with Scarlets, Ospreys, Dragons and WRU-owned Cardiff waiting for the proposals.

Rugby.title: Promising Australian fly-half wants to 'make an impact' with Leicester
Rugby.summary: Western Force fly-half Grace Freeman will ply her trade with Leicester Tigers in the 2026/26 Premiership Women's Rugby season.
Rugby.body:
Western Force fly-half Grace Freeman will ply her trade with Leicester Tigers in the 2026/26 Premiership Women’s Rugby season. The 25-year-old, who previously represented the Melbourne Rebels, returned to Super Rugby Women’s in June after a 450 day layoff with a significant foot injury sustained against the Fijian Drua in the 2024 campaign.

In November, the New Zealand-born playmaker was awarded a national contract by the Wallaroos. She has been the subject of “long-term” interest from Rugby Australia and is expected to make her Test rugby debut in the near future for the green and gold.

Rugby.title: 'Follow me on Instagram': Zackary keeps it coy over club future
Rugby.summary: Kate Zackary may have ended her time with Trailfinders Women, but does that means she’s ending her time in Premiership Women’s Rugby?
Rugby.body:
Kate Zackary may have ended her time with Trailfinders Women, but does that means she’s ending her time in Premiership Women’s Rugby? “Who knows? Follow me on Instagram,” is the flanker’s answer.

For now, Zackary has international duties and winning her 50th cap with USA Women’s Eagles on her mind. The club season may have ended, but the Trailfinders captain is heading to South Africa for the Eagles’ back-to-back tests against the Springbok Women at Ellis Park and Loftus Versfeld.

Rugby.title: Springboks' place at top of world rankings comes under threat
Rugby.summary: Having seen their position at the top of the World Rugby Men's Rankings protected since mid-September, through the Quilter Nations Series and then the Guinness Six Nations, South Africa could be knocked off their perch on the opening weekend of the inaugural Nations Championship.
Rugby.body:
Having seen their position at the top of the World Rugby Men’s Rankings protected since mid-September, through the Quilter Nations Series and then the Guinness Six Nations, South Africa could be knocked off their perch on the opening weekend of the inaugural Nations Championship.

Given that England have never tasted victory at Ellis Park in Johannesburg in the professional era, and their only win there was 18-9 back in 1972, winning this weekend will be a very tall order for Steve Borthwick’s men, who travel without regular captain Maro Itoje.

Rugby.title: Antoine Dupont pulls out of France's Nations Championship
Rugby.summary: Antoine Dupont is a shock withdrawal from France's Nations Championship squad due to a late injury sustained in the knockout stages of Toulouse's victorious run to the Top 14 title.
Rugby.body:
Antoine Dupont is a shock withdrawal from France’s Nations Championship squad due to a late injury sustained in the knockout stages of Toulouse’s victorious run to the Top 14 title.

The scrum-half has been ruled out of the new competition between the Northern and Southern Hemispheres, which starts this coming weekend. While he was already unavailable for France’s opening match against the All Blacks on July 4th, he could have been available for selection against Australia on July 11th and Japan on July 18th.

Rugby.title: AXA Elite 1 Final | Stade Bordelais complete French championship four-peat
Rugby.summary: Four-in-a-row titles are uncommon events and deserve to be celebrated – yet French rugby marked two such rare runs in less than 24 hours.
Rugby.body:
Four-in-a-row titles are uncommon events and deserve to be celebrated – yet French rugby marked two such rare runs in less than 24 hours. It had happened first at Stade de France, on a dark and stormy Saturday night, when Toulouse repeated the back-to-back-to-back-to-back exploits of their 1994/97 vintage to claim their sixth Top 14 crown in seven years.

Stade Bordelais matched them on Sunday evening, beating ASM Romagnat 22-10 at Biarritz’s Stade Aguilera, to add the 2026 AXA Elite 1 championship to the 2023, 2024 and 2025 titles already in their collection.

Rugby.title: Drones, specs and sensors
Rugby.summary: Duncan Locke arms England with the technology and innovation to perform at their best on the pitch.
Rugby.body:
Fifteen years ago, Cristiano Ronaldo was the star of a genre-defying film. At the peak of his powers, the footballer was the subject of 'docu-mmercial' - his skills scientifically unpicked and assessed as part of a promotion for a car engine oil.

It was a success and a key segment went viral., external In it, Ronaldo stood eight yards out from goal and waited for a cross. However, as the ball was in mid-flight, the lights were cut, plunging the penalty box into darkness.

Rugby.title: Springboks pick Libbok at 10 to face England
Rugby.summary: Manie Libbok will start in place of the injured Sacha Feinberg-Mngomezulu at fly-half for South Africa against England on Saturday.
Rugby.body:
Manie Libbok will start in place of the injured Sacha Feinberg-Mngomezulu at fly-half for South Africa against England after the Springboks named their team more than five days before Saturday's kick-off.

Libbok sees off competition from Handre Pollard, who started the Boks' Rugby World Cup final wins in 2019 and 2023, to take the 10 shirt in a formidable side. Libbok's inclusion is one of four changes to the South Africa side that beat Ireland 24-13 in November in their most recent full-strength run-out.

Rugby.title: 'That will certainly add to our motivation this week': Rassie Erasmus
Rugby.summary: South Africa head coach Rassie Erasmus believes landmark occasions for Cheslin Kolbe and Damian Willemse will provide an extra incentive when his side open their Nations Championship campaign against England in Johannesburg on Saturday.
Rugby.body:
South Africa head coach Rassie Erasmus believes landmark occasions for Cheslin Kolbe and Damian Willemse will provide an extra incentive when his side open their Nations Championship campaign against England in Johannesburg on Saturday.

Kolbe and Willemse are both set to earn their 50th Test caps at Ellis Park after being named in an experienced starting XV to face Steve Borthwick’s side. While Erasmus admitted the double milestone adds another layer of motivation, he stressed that sentiment alone will count for little in what he expects to a “hard grind of a match”.

Rugby.title: Kiwi forwards coach Aaron Dundon swaps Ireland for Wales
Rugby.summary: Scarlets have hired the vastly-experienced Aaron Dundon as the region's lead forwards coach ahead of the 2026-27 season.
Rugby.body:
Scarlets have hired the vastly-experienced Aaron Dundon as the region’s lead forwards coach ahead of the 2026-27 season. Born in New Zealand and a former Leinster player, Dundon joins Scarlets from the back-to-back URC champions, having previously worked in Ireland with Ulster and the national U20s team.

Dundon also has coaching experience in France with La Rochelle and back home in the NPC with the Tasman Makos. He will work closely alongside Scarlets’ current specialist lineout coach Albert van de Berg, and Bradley Davies (contact area).

Rugby.title: England nemesis Pollard misses out as Rassie Erasmus names his Bok side
Rugby.summary: Cheslin Kolbe and Damian Willemse will both make their 50th appearances for South Africa on Saturday against England in Johannesburg.
Rugby.body:
Cheslin Kolbe and Damian Willemse will both make their 50th appearances for South Africa on Saturday against England in Johannesburg. The Springboks host Steve Borthwick’s outfit at Ellis Park on Saturday in their first Test match of 2026, and to launch their Nations Championship campaign, with Scotland and Wales to follow.

Despite naming several uncapped players in the recent victory over the Barbarians, Rassie Erasmus has reverted to a tried and tested 23, with only one player in the starting XV possessing fewer than 20 caps – second-row Ruan Nortje.

Rugby.title: Top 10 Junior World Championship record wins: South Africa U20s dominate
Rugby.summary: As defending champions, South Africa put out a statement of intent that they are not going to give up their Junior World Championship title lightly, with a 104-7 opening round win over an out-classed Uruguay.
Rugby.body:
As defending champions, South Africa put out a statement of intent that they are not going to give up their Junior World Championship title lightly with a 104-7 opening round win over an out-classed Uruguay in Tbilisi on Saturday.

While harder battles undoubtedly lay ahead – host nation Georgia are their next opponents on Thursday, it was some display from Kevin Foote’s side who recorded the fourth century, and the first for 13 years, since international age-grade rugby’s elite competition began in 2008.

Rugby.title: Ukrainian women break into the elite division of rugby sevens after victory at the European Championship
Rugby.summary: Ukrainian women break into the elite division of rugby sevens after victory at the European Championship Українські Національні Новини (УНН)
Rugby.body:
In the final of the second round in Chisinau, the Ukrainian women defeated Italy with a score of 10:5. The team earned the right to compete in the elite Championship division next season.

The women's rugby sevens team of Ukraine won the European Championship in the Trophy division, as reported by the National Olympic Committee (NOC) of Ukraine on Monday, writes UNN.

Rugby.title: Morgan backs Lake's Wales captaincy credentials
Rugby.summary: Flanker Jac Morgan says he will back up "brilliant" captain Dewi Lake after the hooker retained the Wales leadership role.
Rugby.body:
Flanker Jac Morgan says he will back up "brilliant" captain Dewi Lake after the hooker retained the Wales leadership role. Lake took over as skipper when Morgan suffered a dislocated left shoulder in the act of scoring against Argentina in November.

The British and Irish Lions flanker has returned to the Wales squad after missing almost two campaigns but head coach Steve Tandy opted to keep Lake as his captain. "I'm just looking forward to backing Dewi up," said Morgan. "He's a great captain, a great leader and someone you'll always follow into a game.

Rugby.summary: "He definitely owns the room when he speaks,'' Taylor told media in Christchurch ahead of the first Test.
Rugby.body:
The All Blacks playing group and new management met in Auckland on Wednesday last week, with only ten days to prepare for the first Test of 2026, against France in Christchurch.

The All Blacks head coach, who returned to New Zealand after leading the Kobelco Kobe Steelers to a Japan Rugby League One title, announced on Sunday that Will Jordan, Tupou Vaa’i, and Wallace Sititi are all available for selection.

Rugby.title: Alex Austerberry: Saracens have 'risen the bar' with latest PWR success
Rugby.summary: Saracens' Director of Women's Rugby, Alex Austerberry, had waited a long time to take the North London club back to the top of English rugby.
Rugby.body:
“We were at the top, I think, slightly longer than Gloucester Hartpury,” Alex Austerberry smirked. Saracens‘ Director of Women’s Rugby had waited a long time to take the North London club back to the top of English rugby. In Premiership Women’s Rugby‘s early days, when it was still Premier 15s and novelty bags of crisps were given as Player of the Match awards, his team were unstoppable.

In 2018 and 2019 his team finished the season with the trophy. Covid-19 curtailed the 2019/20 season, and in 2021 they were beaten by Harlequins at Kingsholm Stadium. That loss was just a speed bump and, one year later, they beat Exeter Chiefs.

Rugby.title: PWR Final | Trailfinders Women player ratings vs Saracens
Rugby.summary: Saracens secured their fourth Premiership Women's Rugby title at Twickenham Stoop this afternoon with a dominant 52-14 win over Trailfinders Women.
Rugby.body:
Saracens secured their fourth Premiership Women’s Rugby title at Twickenham Stoop this afternoon with a dominant 52-14 win over Trailfinders Women. It wasn’t to be for Barney Maddison’s side today. They were second best this afternoon and came up short in the battle up front, but they can be proud of making it to a showpiece finale.

Saracens ran out 52-14 winners in what was Saracens captain, Marlie Packer’s final game for the club as she joins Harlequins next season. It was fitting she ended her Saracens career.

Rugby.title: Saracens win record fourth title with emphatic victory
Rugby.summary: Marlie Packer scores two tries on her final Saracens appearance as the north London side secure a record fourth league title with a comprehensive 52-14 win over Trailfinders at Twickenham Stoop.
Rugby.body:
Marlie Packer scored two tries on her final Saracens appearance as the north London side secured a record fourth league title with a comprehensive 52-14 win over first-time finalists Trailfinders at Twickenham Stoop.

Former England captain Packer, who is joining Harlequins next season after nine years with Saracens, grabbed her double in eight first-half minutes as Alex Austerberry's side dominated their London rivals in the Premiership Women's Rugby final.

Rugby.title: PWR Final | Saracens player ratings vs Trailfinders
Rugby.summary: Three seasons later, Saracens have climbed back to the summit of Premiership Women’s Rugby, overpowering underdogs Trailfinders Women 52–14 to claim their fourth PWR title in front of a packed Stoop.
Rugby.body:
After a four year wait Saracens have climbed back to the summit of Premiership Women’s Rugby, overpowering underdogs Trailfinders Women 52–14 to claim their fourth PWR title in front of a packed Stoop.

Under Alex Austerberry, Saracens were simply dominant from the very first second, controlling the narrative of the game through a level of physicality the opposition couldn’t match.

Rugby.title: PWR Final | Takeaways as Saracens end their title drought against Trailfinders
Rugby.summary: Saracens registered a record score in the PWR Final to claim their first English league title in four years against Trailfinders Women.
Rugby.body:
Saracens registered a record score in the PWR Final to claim their first English league title in four years against Trailfinders Women. Marlie Packer scored a brace of first-half tries at the Twickenham Stoop as the North London club won 52-14. It was a ruthless performance from Alex Austerberry’s team, who scored three close-range tries before the 30 minute mark thanks to Julia Omokhuale and Packer’s dual efforts.

Sydney Gregson extended the Saracens lead ahead of half-time, while Jess Breach and Alysha Corrigan’s scores early in the second 40 minutes largely put the contest to bed. Barney Maddison’s Trailfinders did respond through Maya Monteil and Abi Burton efforts, but they paled in contrast to further Saracens tries from Zoe Harrison and Breach.

Rugby.title: Immanuel Feyi-Waboso can 'be the best winger in the world' says ex-Lions star
Rugby.summary: Anthony Watson is backing “uniquely freakish” England star Immanuel Feyi-Waboso to become the best wing in the world – if he can avoid any more injuries.
Rugby.body:
Anthony Watson is backing “uniquely freakish” England star Immanuel Feyi-Waboso to become the best wing in the world – if he can avoid any more injuries. Feyi-Waboso is poised to start England’s July tour opener against South Africa at Ellis Park on Saturday, putting behind him the run of shoulder, concussion and hamstring issues that have limited him to 13 caps since making his debut in 2024.

Now restored to full fitness after making a successful comeback from a broken jaw in the Prem final for Exeter, Watson believes one of his successors in the number 14 could soon become world class.

Rugby.title: The two areas the Junior Springboks were 'challenged' in 104-7 rout
Rugby.summary: The Junior Springboks scored 16 tries, including a penalty try, to open their Junior World Championship campaign with a 104-7 demolition of Uruguay in Tbilisi on Saturday...
Rugby.body:
The Junior Springboks scored 16 tries, including a penalty try, to open their Junior World Championship campaign with a 104-7 demolition of Uruguay in Tbilisi on Saturday, leading 50-0 at the break and conceding only after the final hooter.

Wing Jordan Steenkamp and flanker Risima Khosa both bagged hat-tricks. Yet despite what appeared to the naked eye to be a total mismatch, SA U20 head coach Kevin Foote was honest about where Uruguay made life difficult.

Rugby.title: Thirty-two of Chile's 37-man Nations Cup squad come from one team
Rugby.summary: Pablo Lemoine has announced Chile’s squad for the July window of the 2026 Nations Cup, with several of the Cóndores' main headliners included in the 37-player squad.
Rugby.body:
Pablo Lemoine has announced Chile’s squad for the July window of the 2026 Nations Cup, with several of the Cóndores’ main headliners included in the 37-player squad. Racing 92’s hooker Diego Escobar makes a return to the team after missing Chile’s last test, in which they lost 34-19 against Italy. Escobar enjoyed a great season for the Ciel et Blanc, featuring in 21 of 32 matches, and the front-rower is expected to bring added consistency to Chile’s set-piece.

Lock Javier Eissmann (SU Agen), loose forward Matías Dittus (CS Annonay), outside back Iñaki Ayarza (US Oyonnax), and utility back Santiago Videla (Chicago Hounds) are the four and last abroad operators that are set to get involved in Chile’s campaign.
