Rugby.title: Fissler Confidential: Ellis Genge to exit English rugby?
Rugby.summary: England and Lions loosehead Ellis Genge is being lined up for a lucrative move to the Top 14 or Japan Rugby League One following the Rugby World Cup in Australia next year.
Rugby.body:
England and Lions loosehead Ellis Genge is being lined up for a lucrative move to the Top 14 or Japan Rugby League One following the Rugby World Cup in Australia next year. It is understood that there is considerable interest from both France and Japan in the Bristol Bears star, who returned to his hometown club from Leicester Tigers after helping them beat Saracens to win their 11th Prem title in 2022.

Genge, who grew up on the Knowle West council estate and has won 80 England caps, signed a long-term contract in September 2024 and will be 32 next February. He was touted for a move to Super League side Warrington Wolves a couple of years ago.

Rugby.title: Max Jorgensen: 'When we're at our best, not many teams can beat us'
Rugby.summary: "You want to keep competing every week to keep your spot, because at the end of day no one owns the jersey."
Rugby.body:
The Wallabies are banking on stability rather than relying on anything special when they try to end their losing streak against Ireland in the upcoming Nations Championship. Ireland have won five straight Tests against Australia, including last year’s 46-19 romp in Dublin, with the Wallabies not tasting success against the world No.3 nation since 2018.

But utility back Max Jorgensen feels the Wallabies are ready to end that losing run, and they’ll get their chance when they host Ireland at Allianz Stadium in Sydney next Saturday.

Rugby.title: Steve Lancaster reveals what Anzac Day Tests mean for Super Rugby Pacific
Rugby.summary: NZR and Rugby Australia announced on Sunday that the three Test matches will all be played at Suncorp Stadium in Brisbane, in 2027, 2029, and 2031.
Rugby.body:
New Zealand Rugby CEO Steve Lancaster has explained what Anzac Day Bledisloe Cup Tests will mean for Super Rugby Pacific, and how the competition’s draw may look in 2027. NZR and Rugby Australia announced on Sunday that the three Test matches will all be played at Suncorp Stadium in Brisbane in 2027, 2029, and 2031.

April 25’s Test match between the Wallabies and the All Blacks will be played during the Super Rugby Pacific season, likely forcing the league to adjust the format of the sixteen- round competition.

Rugby.title: Justin Marshall identifies the key factor behind Kyle Preston's selection
Rugby.summary: The Crusaders halfback featured for Scott Robertson’s All Blacks in the 2025 Rugby Championship Test match against the Springboks, coming off the bench replacing Finlay Christie.
Rugby.body:

Rugby.title: Wales set to reduce squad for Nations Championship
Rugby.summary: Head coach Steve Tandy says the Wales squad for the Nations Championship will be cut to between 32 and 34 players early next week.
Rugby.body:
Wales head coach Steve Tandy says he will reduce his squad for the Nations Championship early next week. Tandy watched his side beat the Barbarians 33-31 at the Allianz Stadium in an uncapped warm-up game before three matches against Fiji, Argentina and South Africa.

Wales face Fiji at the Cardiff City Stadium next Saturday before travelling to play the Pumas and the Springboks. Tandy had initially named a 48-man squad before injuries to Louie Hennessey, Keiron Assiratti and Liam Belcher and will now cut at least 11 more players with the new squad size between 32 and 34.

Rugby.title: Matatu shade Blues, Hurricanes Poua stun Chiefs Manawa
Rugby.summary: Both games were high-scoring affairs, with the Poua clinching their first Aupiki victory in over 120 weeks, holding on for a narrow victory over Chiefs Manawa, 34-29.
Rugby.body:
Super Rugby Aupiki’s All-in weekend at Navigation Homes Stadium in Pukekohe has treated fans to two close encounters, where Matatu and the Hurricanes Poua have come out on top.

Round three of the 2026 Super Rugby Aupiki competition saw the top two teams face off against each other, the Blues, and Matatu, after the two winless sides, Hurricanes Poua, and Chiefs Manawa battled it out.

Rugby.title: 'You guys just pile s*** on players': Ex-Wallaby weighs in on 'easy target' Damian McKenzie
Rugby.summary: Ex-Wallaby hooker Jeremy Paul has weighed in on the criticism aimed at Chiefs and All Black first five Damian McKenzie in the wake of the Super Rugby Pacific final.
Rugby.body:
Ex-Wallaby hooker Jeremy Paul has weighed in on the criticism aimed at Chiefs and All Black first five Damian McKenzie in the wake of the Super Rugby Pacific final. The former Brumby said that the level of expectation on All Blacks is very high and that the criticism comes with the territory of being an All Black.

Paul said that New Zealanders “s*** on” players who don’t perform and that has always been the case. “I think the thing about Damian McKenzie is, unfortunately, he’s been an easy target for a couple of years now,” Paul said on the Good, Bad the Rugby ANZ podcast.

Rugby.title: Trans-Tasman rivals unveil Anzac Day Bledisloe Cup tradition from 2027
Rugby.summary: “Playing the Bledisloe Cup on Anzac Day creates a unique trans-Tasman sporting occasion that honours our countries’ shared history and cultures, while bringing people together through rugby."
Rugby.body:
The three Bledisloe Cup Test matches, supported by the Queensland Government, will be played at Suncorp Stadium in Brisbane. “The inaugural Anzac Day Bledisloe Cup Test will kick-off at a family-friendly early afternoon time slot on Sunday 25 April 2027, creating a global sporting fixture in one of the most significant days on both nations’ calendars.”

The fixture will mean the All Blacks and Wallabies’ international seasons in 2027 will start as early as April. The Super Rugby Pacifc competition format will be revealed in the coming weeks, which will be affected by the Anzac Day fixture.

The fixture will mean the All Blacks and Wallabies’ international seasons in 2027 will start as early as April. The Super Rugby Pacifc competition format will be revealed in the coming weeks, which will be affected by the Anzac Day fixture.

Rugby.title: Stade Toulousain crowned Top 14 champions for 25th time after storm delay
Rugby.summary: Around the 52nd minute, Toulouse struck again through Romain Ntamack, who slotted a penalty kick to stretch the lead to 28–13.
Rugby.body:
Stade Toulousain will remain at the top of the Top14, following another successful final in which they defeated Montpellier 28-20 to earn their 25th Bouclier de Brennus. The defending champions put Montpellier under intense pressure in the opening 10 minutes, even crossing the whitewash in the 6th minute thanks to a spectacular try scored by Peato Mauvaka. The hooker found space out wide and ran unopposed until the in-goal area, gliding past Donovan Taofifenua with a masterful dummy step before grounding the ball.

Montpellier tried to fight back and had every chance to level the score, but were unable to do so, allowing Toulouse to survive a 15-minute onslaught. The best Les Cistes managed was a penalty kick well-taken by Domingo Miotti.

Rugby.title: The Steve Tandy verdict on Baa-Baas win as George North makes candid admission
Rugby.summary: Head coach Steve Tandy declared Wales’ win over the Barbarians a “brilliant hit-out” for their Nations Championship opener with Fiji.
Rugby.body:
Head coach Steve Tandy declared Wales’ win over the Barbarians a “brilliant hit-out” for their Nations Championship opener with Fiji. Wales were without 13 England and France-based players because the Allianz Stadium fixture fell outside World Rugby’s official window and held on for a 33-31 victory.

It was the first time Wales had recorded back-to-back wins since the 2023 World Cup, after ending their Six Nations campaign in March with victory over Italy. Wales now their turn attention to next Saturday’s Cardiff clash against Fiji, which is officially an away game, before concluding the first part of the inaugural Nations Championship with trips to Argentina and South Africa.

Rugby.title: North star shines bright right to the end
Rugby.summary: Even by the extraordinary standards George North has reached during his glittering 16-year career, the Wales great found something special for his farewell match.
Rugby.body:
It was some farewell. Even by some of the extraordinary standards George North has reached during his glittering 16-year career. Two tries for the Barbarians against Wales, which included a blockbuster score with his first touch.

Then his final act as a rugby player came when he kicked his first professional conversion against Wales. Who would have thought that? When North woke up on the morning before his final act, would he have been happy with that scenario?

Rugby.title: South Africa U20 player ratings vs Uruguay | World Rugby Junior World Championships 2026
Rugby.summary: Junior Springboks player ratings: Anything other than a hammering of underdogs Uruguay would have been a moral loss for the Junior Boks and they didn't fail to deliver for coach Kevin Foote.
Rugby.body:
Junior Springboks player ratings: Anything other than a hammering of underdogs Uruguay would have been a moral loss for the Junior Boks and they didn’t fail to deliver for coach Kevin Foote.

Uruguay put up a brave effort but the Baby Boks were just on a different level and, in truth, this was a mismatch from the outset. The final score of 104-7 told its own story. Even so, there were still little details to like, others to tidy up, and the odd dose of brainrot despite a completely one-sided afternoon.

Rugby.title: New-look Wales end losing streak with Barbarians win
Rugby.summary: Amy Williams scores two tries on her first Wales appearance as visitors end a run of nine defeats against a star-studded Baa-Baas.
Rugby.body:
New-look Wales produced a refreshed display as they ended a nine-game losing streak albeit in this non-cap friendly against Barbarians at Allianz Stadium. Uncapped wing Amy Williams scored twice between tries from makeshift fly-half Kayleigh Powell and rising scrum-half star Seren Lockwood.

Courtney Keight crossed before another new prospect, Jorja Aiono, added a sixth try late on as Wales did well to last the distance in scorching heat at Twickenham where temperatures hit 31C at kick-off.

Courtney Keight crossed before another new prospect, Jorja Aiono, added a sixth try late on as Wales did well to last the distance in scorching heat at Twickenham where temperatures hit 31C at kick-off.

Rugby.title: Three takeaways as Wales register a 34-17 win against the Barbarians
Rugby.summary: Sean Lynn's Wales have experienced victory for the first time in 11 months thanks to a 27-17 win against the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium.
Rugby.body:
Sean Lynn’s Wales have experienced victory for the first time in 11 months thanks to a 34-17 win against the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium. As the sun began its descent over West London, tries from Kayleigh Powell, Seren Lockwood, Courtney Keight and an Amy Williams brace was enough to overcome a dogged Barbarians side.

The de facto hosts saw Amy Rule, Bryony Cleall and Celia Quansah dot down. Welsh positives have been hard to come by for several years. All of a sudden Sean Lynn’s team have one. Not too many people knew of Jorja Aiono at the start of this season. Now it is hard to imagine a Wales pack without the teenage lock. Even if she only has five caps.

Rugby.title: Rugby greats gather for celebration of Scott Hastings’ life
Rugby.summary: The former British and Irish Lion passed away on May 17 aged 61.
Rugby.body:
Scottish rugby greats have gathered in Edinburgh for a service in memory of Scott Hastings, who died earlier this year. The British and Irish Lions centre passed away on May 17 aged 61, following complications arising from his cancer treatment.

Hasting won 65 caps in an 11-year Test career that began in 1986 and was part of the most recent Scotland team to win the Grand Slam in 1990. On a warm Saturday afternoon, members of the rugby community gathered at George Watson’s College – Hastings’ former school – for a celebration of his life.

Rugby.title: Wales pip the Barbarians despite try double from George North
Rugby.summary: Wales won successive games for the first time since the 2023 World Cup with a 33-31 victory over the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium.
Rugby.body:
Wales won successive games for the first time since the 2023 World Cup with a 33-31 victory over the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium – despite the efforts of George North. After beating Italy in March to end a three-year wait for a Six Nations victory, Wales will head into the upcoming inaugural Nations Championship with confidence stocks replenished further despite being without their English and French-based players.

Outside-half Dan Edwards contributed 14 points with two tries and a couple of conversions, while Kieran Hardy, Reuben Morgan-Williams and Ellis Mee also crossed. Sam Costelow added two conversions.

Rugby.title: Wales player ratings vs Barbarians | 2026
Rugby.summary: Wales have warmed up for their Nations Championship opener against Fiji in Cardiff next week with a 33-31 win against the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium.
Rugby.body:
Wales have warmed up for their Nations Championship opener against Fiji in Cardiff next week with a 33-31 win against the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium. Steve Tandy’s team were pushed hard in a sweltering West London by Scott Robertson’s scratch side, who saw George North cross the whitewash twice in his final match before retirement.

Dan Edward’s two tries went a long way to securing Wales a win, as Kieran Hardy, Reuben Morgan Williams and Ellis Mee also dotted down. Next week Wales’ squad will contain players that ply their trade in the Gallagher Prem and France’s Top 14, but could some of today’s performances have landed more Wales-based talent in the matchday 23 next weekend at Cardiff City Stadium?

Rugby.title: Wales beat Barbarians as North signs off in style
Rugby.summary: Wales beat Barbarians 33-31 at the Allianz Stadium in Twickenham but the day belongs to two-try George North in his last rugby match.
Rugby.body:
Wales warmed up for the Nations Championship with victory against Barbarians on a day when George North delivered a crowd-pleasing, try-scoring farewell. In sweltering sunshine, with temperatures at Allianz Stadium reaching 32C (90F), Wales scored five tries through fly-half Dan Edwards, two, scrum-halves Kieran Hardy and Reuben Morgan-Williams as well as wing Ellis Mee.

But the day belonged to Wales great North who bowed out in style in his final game before retirement with two Barbarians tries. After coming on as a second-half replacement for the Baa-Baas, North's opening try came with his first touch and he also added a conversion with the final kick of the game.

Rugby.title: New Zealand U20 player ratings vs Japan | World Rugby Junior World Championship 2026
Rugby.summary: New Zealand U20 player ratings: The Baby Blacks bagged a 17-point win after their side landed a 38-21 victory, with Mika Muliaina, Bradley Tocker and Jake Frost performing at a high level.
Rugby.body:
New Zealand U20 player ratings: The Baby Blacks bagged a 17-point win after their side landed a 38-21 victory, with Mika Muliaina, Bradley Tocker and Jake Frost performing at a high level.

Despite taking an early lead, New Zealand struggled to put down a relentless Japan side, which levelled the score before the break. However, after Mika Muliaina served ten minutes in the sin-bin, the men in black managed to overcome Japan, adding three more tries before the game was called off in the 68th minute due to a thunderstorm.

Rugby.title: World Rugby statement: New Zealand U20 vs Japan U20 abandoned
Rugby.summary: New Zealand’s World Rugby U20 Championship clash with Japan in Kutaisi was abandoned after nearby lightning strikes forced a suspension of play.
Rugby.body:
New Zealand’s World Rugby U20 Championship clash with Japan in Kutaisi was abandoned after nearby lightning strikes forced a suspension of play. The game will not restart World Rugby have confirmed, with the score confirmed at 38-21 to New Zealand.

The World Rugby statement reads: “Following lightning strikes close to the AIA Arena in Kutaisi the match between New Zealand and Japan was suspended and will not restart. In accordance with the tournament rules the final score will stand as New Zealand 38-21 Japan.

Rugby.title: Ireland U20 player ratings vs England | World Rugby Junior World Championship 2026
Rugby.summary: Ireland U20 player ratings: Ireland’s early blitz had England rocking, and for a brief spell it looked like raw speed might produce a bit of scoreboard blowout.
Rugby.body:
Ireland U20 player ratings: Ireland’s early blitz had England rocking, and for a brief spell it looked like raw speed might produce a bit of scoreboard blowout. But England’s middle period—built on power, crisp hands and a dominant lineout—proved decisive. Ireland’s scrum and finishing on the edge kept it alive, yet their own errors, especially at in front of England try-line, proved terminal.

The 34-27 final score will irk Ireland, as they’ll think they should have won this one. Anchored a scrum that became a genuine weapon, even down a man at times. Huge credit for helping Ireland flip that area of the game.

Rugby.title: England U20 outmuscle Ireland in World Rugby Junior World Championship thriller
Rugby.summary: Connacht winger Daniel Ryan scored a hat-trick but still ended up on the losing side as England opened their 2026 Junior World Championship campaign with a 34-27 victory over Ireland in Tbilisi, Georgia.
Rugby.body:
Connacht winger Daniel Ryan scored a hat-trick but still ended up on the losing side as England opened their 2026 Junior World Championship campaign with a 34-27 victory over Ireland in Tbilisi, Georgia.

Ireland started strongly with two tries in the opening six minutes, but two yellow cards opened the door and England stormed through it, scoring 24 unanswered points. Ireland, who have won their last four opening games in the tournament, made the perfect start in the first minute when Charlie O’Shea converted a try he set up with a lovely kick to Ryan.

Rugby.title: England U20 player ratings vs Ireland | World Rugby Junior World Championship 2026
Rugby.summary: England U20 player ratings: Ireland got out of the gate the fastest, with two tries inside eight minutes, but England struck back with both forward power and a back division that moved the ball with real intent.
Rugby.body:
England U20 player ratings: Ireland got out of the gate the fastest, with two tries inside eight minutes, but England struck back with both forward power and a back division that moved the ball with real intent.

England won the contact zone in the first half, with Ireland struggling to live with that physicality, although the scrum told a different story. Ireland battled hard in the second half, but inaccuracy and discipline cost them as England ran out 34-27 winners.

Rugby.title: Japan XV starlet shines early but Maori All Blacks storm home in Nagoya
Rugby.summary: The Maori All Blacks and Japan XV met in Nagoya for the latest chapter in what has become a frequently visited rivalry in recent years, and Paloma Mizuho Stadium played host to a dramatic game of two halves.
Rugby.body:
The Maori All Blacks and Japan XV met in Nagoya for the latest chapter in what has become a frequently visited rivalry in recent years, and Paloma Mizuho Stadium played host to a dramatic game of two halves.

Japan XV No.10 Ryunosuke Ito put in an electric performance for his side, beating five defenders with three try assists in the first half, capitalising on the visitors being down to 13 men for the final 10 minutes of the period.

Rugby.title: No further injuries for Ireland before Wallabies opener
Rugby.summary: Forwards coach Paul O'Connell says Ireland have no further injury concerns before next week's Nations Championship opener against Australia in Sydney.
Rugby.body:
Forwards coach Paul O'Connell has confirmed Ireland have no further injury concerns before next Saturday's Nations Championship opener against Australia in Sydney. Having missed out on reclaiming the Six Nations title in March, Ireland take on the Wallabies and Japan in Australia and New Zealand in Auckland on 4, 11 and 18 July.

Head coach Andy Farrell's preparations have been hit by injuries to key players including fly-half Jack Crowley, prop Andrew Porter and captain Caelan Doris. While Munster forward Tadhg Beirne and Ulster centre Stuart McCloskey have been struggling with injuries, O'Connell confirmed "everyone is fine".

Rugby.title: 22 World Cup stars included in Springboks squad for Eagles Test matches
Rugby.summary: Springbok Women head coach Swys de Bruin has named a 30-player squad for the team's two-Test series against the USA Women's Eagles.
Rugby.body:
Springbok Women head coach Swys de Bruin has named a 30-player squad for the team’s two-Test series against the USA Women’s Eagles. Both fixtures form part of doubleheaders with the Springbok Men’s Nations Championship fixtures against England and Scotland at Ellis Park [Saturday 4 July] and Loftus Versfeld [Saturday 11 July].

In total, 22 of the squad named by the 66-year-old were part of the squad that reached the quarter-final stage of Women’s Rugby World Cup 2025, where they were beaten by the six-time world champion New Zealand Black Ferns.

Rugby.title: Sale's Perris-Redding given Eagles captaincy for South Africa tour
Rugby.summary: Sale Sharks forward George Perris-Redding will skipper the USA Women's Eagles on their first-ever tour of South Africa.
Rugby.body:
Sale Sharks forward George Perris-Redding will skipper the USA Women’s Eagles on their first-ever tour of South Africa. The 29-year-old takes over the captaincy from her club teammate, Erica Jarrell-Searcy, who is unavailable for the tour fixtures against the Women’s Rugby World Cup 2025 quarterfinalists.

“It’s an important time to develop multiple leaders in the build up to 2029/2033 RWCs,” Jack Hanratty, USA Women’s Eagles head coach, said. “Although Georgie might be taking over the official captaincy for the first time she has been a leader for the Eagles for many years and I know she will lead from the front.”
Rugby.copy:
Sale Sharks forward George Perris-Redding will skipper the USA Women’s Eagles on their first-ever tour of South Africa. The 29-year-old takes over the captaincy from her club teammate, Erica Jarrell-Searcy, who is unavailable for the tour fixtures against the Women’s Rugby World Cup 2025 quarterfinalists.

“It’s an important time to develop multiple leaders in the build up to 2029/2033 RWCs,” Jack Hanratty, USA Women’s Eagles head coach, said. “Although Georgie might be taking over the official captaincy for the first time she has been a leader for the Eagles for many years and I know she will lead from the front.”

Rugby.title: 'He is immature': Jamie George criticises team-mate for 'unacceptable' incident
Rugby.summary: Jamie George has criticised his Saracens team-mate Totoa Auvaa’s “unacceptable” behaviour during the nightclub incident
Rugby.body:
which led to cricketers Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson being dropped by England but insisted he was “a good kid”. England international George described the 21-year-old Samoan back-row as “a rabbit in the headlights in London” and said the academy player “doesn’t know right from wrong”.

Auvaa avoided formal sanctions over what took place at the Rex Rooms in Chelsea in the early hours of June 8, with Saracens calling the events “regrettable for all parties involved” and saying they remained “supportive” of their player.

Rugby.title: George criticises Auvaa's 'unacceptable behaviour' after nightclub incident
Rugby.summary: Jamie George has criticised his Saracens' team-mate Totoa Auvaa's "unacceptable" behaviour after the Samoan was involved in a nightclub incident with England cricket captain Ben Stokes and bowler Gus Atkinson.
Rugby.body:
Jamie George has criticised his Saracens' team-mate Totoa Auvaa's "unacceptable" behaviour after the Samoan was involved in a nightclub incident with England cricket captain Ben Stokes and bowler Gus Atkinson.

Stand-in England rugby captain George, who has 110 caps, said the academy player is like "a rabbit in the headlights in London" and the 21-year-old "doesn't know right from wrong" but insisted he was a "good kid".

Rugby.title: Fijian Drua playoffs-bound once again thanks to Reds blowout
Rugby.summary: Varanisese Qoro has steered Fijiana Drua to a fifth consecutive Super Rugby Women's finals berth by thumping cellar dwellers Queensland 50-17.
Rugby.body:
Varanisese Qoro has steered Fijiana Drua to a fifth consecutive Super Rugby Women’s finals berth by thumping cellar dwellers Queensland 50-17. Knowing that a win at Ballymore Stadium on Saturday would seal a top-four finish, the Drua charged out of the blocks and never looked back.

Flyhalf Qoro was at the centre of it, scoring a try, assisting two more and flexing her blistering speed to move the Drua 11 points clear of the fifth-placed Reds (two) with one round remaining.

Rugby.title: What makes Kyle Preston an outlier among new All Blacks selections
Rugby.summary: Kyle Preston may not be an aspiring debutant, but Monday's All Blacks squad naming was the first time he's been officially selected for New Zealand, earning his place ahead of some of the country's more established names.
Rugby.body:
Kyle Preston may not be an aspiring debutant, but Monday’s All Blacks squad naming was the first time he’s been officially selected in a New Zealand squad, earning his place ahead of some of the country’s more established names.

Last year, Preston got his chance when Cam Roigard, Cortez Ratima, and Noah Hotham were all injured, coming off the bench for Finlay Christie against South Africa. This year, he has jumped at least the latter two on that list to be selected in Dave Rennie’s first All Blacks squad.

Rugby.title: Ruby Tui: 'Just let me put the boots on and see how it all goes'
Rugby.summary: Ruby Tui joked that Saturday’s outing for the Barbarians will not be her first game of rugby in almost 12 months.
Rugby.body:
Ruby Tui joked that Saturday’s outing for the Barbarians will not be her first game of rugby in almost 12 months. The 2021 Rugby World Cup winner instead insisted that she has played “on the low and in private”, although the 24/7 nature of social media has made it difficult to keep all her lowkey appearances a total secret.

Instead, the 34-year-old brands the Allianz Stadium outing as her first “public appearance” in almost a year. That ‘last’ match came last July when Tui scored a try from the bench for the Black Ferns in a 37-12 win against the Wallaroos in the second Laurie O’Reilly Cup clash of the season.

Rugby.title: Aaron Smith gives 'bite the mouthguard' description of Dave Rennie's coaching
Rugby.summary: Aaron Smith is now 15 years removed from his time under New Zealand's new head coach, Dave Rennie, at Manawatu, and even further removed from his 2008 U20 experience with the coach, but the impression Rennie left remains vivid.
Rugby.body:
Aaron Smith is now 15 years removed from his time under New Zealand’s new head coach Dave Rennie at Manawatu, and even further removed from his 2008 U20 experience under the coach, but the impression Rennie left remains vivid.

The 37-year-old, who has played under ex-All Blacks coaches Steve Hansen and Ian Foster at Toyota Verblitz for the past three seasons, has squared off with Rennie’s Kobelco Kobe Steelers throughout his time in Japan Rugby League One, falling short against the eventual champions twice in the recent season.

Rugby.title: New Zealand U20s on patchy start to 2026 ahead of Junior World Championship
Rugby.summary: In a four-20-minute quarters hitout, with neither team wearing numbers and rolling substitutions, Georgia beat New Zealand 22-19 in a warm-up before this week's World Rugby Junior Championship.
Rugby.body:
In a four-20-minute quarters hitout, with neither team wearing numbers and rolling substitutions, Georgia beat New Zealand 22-19 in a warm-up before this week’s World Rugby Junior Championship.

New Zealand coach Kane Jury was more concerned about trialling new combinations and ideas than the score. “For me, it was important we had a hitout,” Jury told RugbyPass. “It was important that we knew where our game stood and how our new players fit into it. We know how good we are and where we want to.”

Rugby.title: Kieran Hardy identifies Bok who has ‘point to prove’ when Barbarians face Wales
Rugby.summary: Kieran Hardy feels Faf de Klerk “has something to prove” as he steps out of the Wales shadows for an Allianz Stadium duel with the two-time World Cup winner.
Rugby.body:
Kieran Hardy feels Faf de Klerk “has something to prove” as he steps out of the Wales shadows for an Allianz Stadium duel with the two-time World Cup winner. Hardy has the chance to impress against the Barbarians on Saturday, with the fixture falling outside World Rugby’s designated Test window and British and Irish Lions scrum-half Tomos Williams among the England and France-based players unavailable to Wales.

The 31-times capped Osprey lines up against De Klerk, who is back on the international stage just days after missing out on South Africa’s squad for the upcoming Nations Championship.

Rugby.title: Grand Slam winning Ireland head coach Philip Doyle dies aged 61
Rugby.summary: Former Ireland head coach Philip Doyle has died at the age of 61.
Rugby.body:
Former Ireland head coach Philip Doyle has died at the age of 61. The news was announced by Blackrock College RFC, where Doyle played and coached over the decades. Doyle, who was affectionately nicknamed ‘Goose’, also enjoyed two spells as Ireland Women’s head coach. The first came between 2003 and 2006, although his second stint at the helm between 2010 and 2014 was his most successful.

That four year tenure included a 2013 Women’s Six Nations Grand Slam and the team’s first-ever win against the Black Ferns at the 2014 Women’s Rugby World Cup in France. Doyle led Ireland to the semi-finals of that tournament before he resigned. Along with his wife, Nicola, Doyle is credited with pioneering women’s rugby at Blackrock College RFC.

Rugby.title: Former Ireland Women head coach Doyle dies aged 61
Rugby.summary: Philip Doyle, who led Ireland to the Women's Six Nations Grand Slam as head coach in 2013, has died aged 61.
Rugby.body:
Philip Doyle, who led Ireland to the Women's Six Nations Grand Slam as head coach in 2013, has died aged 61. He had two spells in the role, leading the side for three years after being appointed as Kevin West's successor in 2003 before returning in 2010.

Doyle enjoyed success in the second stint as he steered Ireland to a first Six Nations Grand Slam in 2013, a first win over New Zealand and a fourth-placed finish at the 2014 World Cup.

Rugby.title: Phillips emulates father to make Barbarians history
Rugby.summary: Carys Phillips and Rowland Phillips will become the first father and daughter to represent the famous club when she faces Wales on Saturday.
Rugby.body:
When Carys Phillips takes the field at the Allianz Stadium on Saturday she will complete a moment of Barbarians history. The Phillips family has already produced two Wales internationals but Carys and Rowland will become the first father and daughter to represent the famous club.

Hooker Phillips, among the most-capped women players for Wales, will follow in the footsteps of her father Rowland - himself a former dual-code international - who wore the black and white jersey some 23 years ago.

Rugby.title: Trailfinders have no fear in PWR final
Rugby.summary: England's Abi Burton says Trailfinders are "not scared" heading into their first Premiership Women's Rugby final against Saracens.
Rugby.body:
Trailfinders forward Abi Burton says her team will have "no fear" heading into their maiden Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) final on Sunday. The west London side secured their highest ever PWR finish, in fourth, before beating triple champions and table-toppers Gloucester-Hartpury in their semi-final.

Now, they meet north London rivals Saracens at The Stoop, with Sarries targeting a record fourth title since the Premier 15s/PWR's inception in 2017. "We ain't scared", Burton told BBC Radio London. "Obviously we've played them twice (in the regular season), and the scorelines haven't gone our way. But there's not an ounce of fear in our dressing room.

Rugby.title: England scrum-half Packer signs new Harlequins deal
Rugby.summary: England World Cup winner Lucy Packer has signed a new undisclosed deal at Harlequins.
Rugby.body:
England scrum-half and World Cup winner Lucy Packer has signed a new deal with Harlequins Women. The 26-year-old made her Quins debut in 2018 and passed 100 appearances for the club in February.

She was part of the side that won the Allianz Premier15s title in 2021 and has 45 caps for England, helping them win the 2025 World Cup and, most recently, the 2026 Six Nations.

Rugby.title: Australia prop Bell ready for reunion with Irish friends for life
Rugby.summary: Angus Bell has said he is ready to face his former Ulster team-mates, who he calls "friends for life", when Australia face Ireland on 4 July.
Rugby.body:
When Australia prop Angus Bell comes up against Ireland, he will be facing some "friends for life". The 25-year-old spent eight months in Northern Ireland playing for Ulster in the United Rugby Championship as he joined on a short-term deal from the Waratahs.

But after leaving Ulster after May's Challenge Cup final defeat by Montpellier in Bilbao, he is set for a swift reunion with some of his former team-mates. Ireland take on the Wallabies in Sydney in their Nations Championship opener on 4 July.

Rugby.title: Georgia add Top 14 star as 40-man Nations Cup squad named
Rugby.summary: Georgia is travelling to South America with a top-level roster, as La Rochelle’s Davit Niniashvili, Exeter’s Bachuki Tchumbadze and Bristol’s Luka Ivanishvili are among the 40 players...
Rugby.body:
Georgia is travelling to South America with a top-level roster, as La Rochelle’s Davit Niniashvili, Exeter’s Bachuki Tchumbadze and Bristol’s Luka Ivanishvili are among the 40 players selected for the opening three rounds of the Nations Cup.

After narrowly missing out on their 18th Men’s Rugby Europe Championship title, the Lelos are set to embark on a new era under Frenchman Pierre-Henri Broncan, who assumed control of the senior men’s team in June.

Rugby.title: Injury hit Romania get timely boost ahead for the Nations Cup
Rugby.summary: After more than nine months off due to injury, Marius Simionescu is set to feature for Romania, as the fullback was one of the 32 players called up for the Nations Cup.
Rugby.body:
After more than nine months off due to injury, Marius Simionescu is set to feature for Romania, as the fullback was one of the 32 players called up for the Nations Cup. David Gérard will have at his disposal a strong roster to face Chile, Uruguay and Samoa, with Simionescu among the most notable returnees, alongside Hinckley Vaovasa (SO Chambéry), Jason Tomane (Baia Mare) and Adrian Mitu (Soyaux-Angoulême XV).

Simionescu was one of the few shining lights for the Stejarii at the 2023 Rugby World Cup, with his attacking prowess providing a rare spark for a side that ultimately suffered four defeats.

Rugby.title: 'One of the best scrum-halves in the game' pens new Harlequins deal
Rugby.summary: Red Roses scrum-half Lucy Packer has re-signed with Premiership Women's Rugby club Harlequins.
Rugby.body:
Red Roses scrum-half Lucy Packer has re-signed with Premiership Women’s Rugby club Harlequins. The 26-year-old Women’s Rugby World Cup 2025 winner made her debut for the West London club in 2018 and made her 100th appearance for Ross Chisholm’s side in February this year.

Packer was a member of the Harlequins team that won a Premier 15s crown in 2021. At this year’s Guinness Women’s Six Nations, as John Mitchell’s England won an eighth title in a row, started all five matches at the Championship. This included a 78-minute performance in Bordeaux against France to secure a fifth Grand Slam on the trot.

Rugby.summary: Wales hooker Carys Phillips will follow in the footsteps of her dad Rowland when she runs out for the Barbarians on Saturday (17:00 BST).
Rugby.body: Wales hooker Carys Phillips will follow in the footsteps of her dad Rowland when she runs out for the Barbarians on Saturday (17:00 BST). Phillips starts against her international team mates as part of a double-header with the men's sides at Allianz Stadium, Twickenham.

Rugby.title: Why Wales see the value in Barbarians double-header
Rugby.summary: Wales men and women hunt rare victories when taking on the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium in Twickenham.
Rugby.body:
There will be plenty of empty seats at Allianz Stadium on Saturday but Wales' glass is half full for their double-header with the Barbarians. After a 2025-26 season featuring a combined tally of just three wins in 19 international fixtures, Steve Tandy's men (14:00 BST) and Sean Lynn's women (17:00 BST) each get the chance for a morale-boosting success in Twickenham.

No caps will be awarded at the home of English rugby, where Wales are the away sides, and a crowd of around 20,000 is expected at an 82,000-capacity stadium. Nonetheless, both Welsh bosses insist there is plenty at stake.

Rugby.title: Liz Crake: 'I’ve been there, done it, made mistakes, had big decisions'
Rugby.summary: Liz Crake is gearing up for the Premiership Women's Rugby Final at the Twickenham Stoop on Sunday 28 July.
Rugby.body:
“I didn’t necessarily see, bigger, stronger women, growing up,” Liz Crake commented, as she sheltered from the sweltering London sun. Crake is gearing up for the Premiership Women’s Rugby Final, at the Twickenham Stoop, and pulls up the itinerary for Sunday’s clash between Saracens and Trailfinders Women.

“It’s supposed to be 26,” she noted, scrolling through her phone. “Light rain showers possible, and some wind.” Making the league’s ‘Battle of The Best’ decider is a scenario Crake would have hoped for when she returned to Saracens, from Trailfinders, at the start of the season. This was the club where it all started at 15. Crake would ultimately make a name for herself at Wasps, taking on the captaincy there, before the club’s financial woes forced her to look elsewhere.

Rugby.title: Who makes the most-capped U20 graduate XV
Rugby.summary: Since 2008, when the U20 age category was first used for international rugby's elite 'next-gen' competition, over 1,000 players have played in the Junior World Championship and subsequently gone on to be capped by their countries at senior level.
Rugby.body:
For the players from 16 teams currently gathered together in Georgia ahead of the start of the 2026 Junior World Championship, there is an opportunity to follow in the footsteps of some of the greats of the modern game as well as bagging some silverware early on in their careers.

Since 2008, when the U20 age category was first used for international rugby’s elite ‘next-gen’ competition, the tournament has a proven track record in developing young talent into Test-ready stars. Over 1,000 players have played in the Junior World Championship and subsequently gone on to be capped by their countries at senior level,

Rugby.title: Four key battles to look out for in the 2026 PWR Final
Rugby.summary: Another historic Premiership Women's Rugby Final approaches, as Saracens and Trailfinders Women go head to head on Sunday 28 June at the Twickenham Stoop.
Rugby.body:
Another historic Premiership Women’s Rugby Final approaches, as Saracens and Trailfinders Women go head to head on Sunday 28 June at the Twickenham Stoop. After two box office semi-finals, where Exeter Chiefs pushed Saracens to the very limit, leading up until the 74th minute only to come away defeated 40-38 as a late Jess Breach try put daylight between the two teams.

Meanwhile, Trailfinders stunned reigning PWR champions and three-peaters Gloucester Hartpury on the road 29-26. Knockout rugby certainly delivered and now Trailfinders enter their first Final, in only their third season in PWR, against six-time finalists Saracens.

Rugby.title: James Pater: 'Teams want to play England
Rugby.summary: Likened to George Furbank, Northampton's teenage talent made his Prem debut while still at school and will be a key man for England U20s.
Rugby.body:
James Pater was still at school when he made his Gallagher Prem debut. The venue was a sold-out Kingsholm. Pater was still five weeks from turning 18. The tall, willowy playmaker had yet to partake in a senior game at any level, never mind turn out in the country’s elite division. Yet status and experience matter little to Phil Dowson and Sam Vesty, the coaching brains trust who have just taken Northampton Saints to a second Prem crown in three years. To them, Pater was ready.

“It was in the middle of my A-levels, during half-term,” the teenager says. “We were training Tuesday and Thursday, and found out on the Thursday I was on the bench. It all happened so quickly it didn’t even register. I didn’t have time to overthink it.

“It was in the middle of my A-levels, during half-term,” the teenager says. “We were training Tuesday and Thursday, and found out on the Thursday I was on the bench. It all happened so quickly it didn’t even register. I didn’t have time to overthink it.

Rugby.title: James Pater: 'Teams want to play England
Rugby.summary: Likened to George Furbank, Northampton's teenage talent made his Prem debut while still at school and will be a key man for England U20s.
Rugby.body:
James Pater was still at school when he made his Gallagher Prem debut. The venue was a sold-out Kingsholm. Pater had turned 18 just three months earlier. The tall, willowy playmaker had yet to partake in a senior game at any level, never mind turn out in the country’s elite division. Yet status and experience matter little to Phil Dowson and Sam Vesty, the coaching brains trust who have just taken Northampton Saints to a second Prem crown in three years. Rugby.summary: Premiership Women’s Rugby will complete the circle it started when the competition launched in 2017/18 when Saracens take on Trailfinders Women in this season’s PWR Final at the Twickenham Stoop on Sunday.
Rugby.body:
Premiership Women’s Rugby will complete the circle it started when the competition launched in 2017/18 when Saracens take on Trailfinders Women in this season’s PWR Final at the Twickenham Stoop on Sunday.

That first season Saracens lifted what was then the Premier 15s trophy when they defeated Harlequins 24-20 in the final at Trailfinders Sports Ground. Five years before the team that now call the venue home came into being.

Rugby.title: Nathan Hughes sent public message after Nations Championship omission
Rugby.summary: Former England No.8 Nathan Hughes has been assured “the door is always open” as he waits to make his Fiji debut despite having missed out on the final 32-strong Fiji Water Flying Fijians...
Rugby.body:
Former England No.8 Nathan Hughes has been assured “the door is always open” as he waits to make his Fiji debut despite having missed out on the final 32-strong Fiji Water Flying Fijians squad for the three matches in the Nations Championships.

Interim Fiji head coach Senirusi Seruvakula is preparing the powerful squad, with significant European-based talent, to face Wales in Cardiff on July 5, England at Everton’s ground on July 11 and Scotland at Murrayfield on July 18.

Rugby.title: World Rugby U20 Junior World Championships – 9 Players to Watch
Rugby.summary: There are a wealth of gifted players from 16 countries who are sure to get fans off their seats over the next three weeks in Georgia
Rugby.body:
The 2026 U20 Junior World Championship kicks off in Georgia and there will be a plethora of super-skilled next-gen stars on show over the coming weeks. The following list is just a smattering of the richly-talented individuals on show and many unheralded players will undoubtedly emerge before the Finals day on Saturday, 18 July.

If you like rugby and live in England and haven’t heard of Lucas Friday then let us know what life is like under the rock. For a guy who’s done two ACLs in the last three seasons he’s achieved a helluva lot – 15 Prem and European apps for Harlequins this season for one. He was aged just 17 when he embarked on a World Rugby U20 Champion campaign in Cape Town alongside the likes of Henry Pollock and Junior Kpoku. Quietly composed and commanding, he kicks superbly off both feet, and his future battle with Archie McParland for the England senior 9 jersey should captivate fans for years to come.

Rugby.title: Saracens' giant Totoa Auva'a off the hook for nightclub brawl
Rugby.summary: Saracens lock Totoa Auvaa, who was at the centre of a bust-up with England fast bowler Gus Atkinson, has been told that he will face no disciplinary action for his Chelsea nightclub scrap.
Rugby.body:
Saracens lock Totoa Auva’a, who was at the centre of a bust-up with England fast bowler Gus Atkinson, has been told that he will face no disciplinary action for his Chelsea nightclub scrap.

Highly rated Samoan-born prospect Auva’a, 21, who plays lock or blindside flanker, attempted to punch Atkinson while the England cricket team were out celebrating a win over New Zealand, but missed and hit an England and Wales Cricket Board security guard instead.

Rugby.title: 'It's a shame': Wales camp react as Christ Tshiunza 'deemed unavailable'
Rugby.summary: Wales boss Steve Tandy admits it is a “shame” new Sale signing Christ Tshiunza will be unavailable because of the Welsh Rugby Union’s 25-cap ruling.
Rugby.body:
Wales boss Steve Tandy admits it is a “shame” new Sale signing Christ Tshiunza will be unavailable because of the Welsh Rugby Union’s 25-cap ruling. The 24-year-old flanker will end his five-year stay at Exeter – where he began his Test career and the ruling did not affect him as an uncapped player – this summer and head north to Manchester, despite having had interest from Welsh sides Cardiff and Ospreys.

The WRU selection policy requires players that represent clubs outside Wales to have won at least 25 caps, but Tshiunza is on 15 and will be unable to play international rugby next season in the build-up to the 2027 World Cup.

Rugby.title: Sleepless nights and inner torment: Marcus Smith opens up on England career
Rugby.summary: The Harlequins playmaker remains part of Steve Borthwick’s squad for next month’s Nations Championship matches.
Rugby.body:
Marcus Smith will continue riding his England roller coaster as he reflects with pride on a career that has caused sleepless nights spent ruminating on mistakes. Smith won his 50th cap in the final match of the Six Nations against France and remains part of Steve Borthwick’s squad for next month’s Nations Championship matches against South Africa, Fiji and Argentina.

Seemingly destined from 2022 to act as England’s fly-half for the foreseeable future, the electric Harlequins playmaker was instead converted to full-back and has since been remoulded as a bench option who covers both positions.

Rugby.summary: The Nations Championship kicks off next week, and between now and then, Wallabies players will have their final opportunities to impress coach Joe Schmidt for matchday selection.
Rugby.body:
The Nations Championship kicks off next week, and between now and then, Wallabies players will have their final opportunities to impress coach Joe Schmidt for matchday selection.

A hallmark of the first Wallabies squad of 2026 was that it was mostly picked on red-hot Super Rugby Pacific or other club form, and Queensland Reds fullback Jock Campbell is no exception to that criterion.

Rugby.summary: There are some new faces in the Australia U20 team named to play Spain in their opening match of the World Rugby Junior World Championship, headlined by rising Queensland Reds star Treyvon Pritchard.
Rugby.body:
There are some new faces in the Australia U20 team named to play Spain in their opening match of the World Rugby Junior World Championship, headlined by rising Queensland Reds star Treyvon Pritchard.

The 12-cap Reds flyer has been named in the No.12 jersey, with France-based pair Lehopoame Leota and Kingbenjamin Swerling-Finaipepe also in line for potential U20 debuts off the bench.

Rugby.title: Scarlets sign New Zealand forward Grace
Rugby.summary: Once-capped New Zealand forward Cullen Grace will join Scarlets from Super Rugby side Crusaders in October.
Rugby.body:
Scarlets have signed New Zealand back-rower Cullen Grace from Super Rugby side Crusaders. The 26-year-old has made one Test appearance for the All Blacks, against Australia in 2020, while he has also represented the Maori All Blacks on three occasions.

Grace will miss the start of the 2026-27 United Rugby Championship (URC) season and link up with Scarlets in October, following the completion of Canterbury's National Provincial Championship campaign later this year.

Rugby.title: Saracens' Auvaa not punished for Stokes incident
Rugby.summary: Saracens forward Totoa Auvaa is not sanctioned after he was involved in a nightclub incident with England cricket captain Ben Stokes.
Rugby.body:
Saracens forward Totoa Auvaa will not be sanctioned after he was involved in a nightclub incident with England cricket captain Ben Stokes and bowler Gus Atkinson. Stokes and Atkinson were made unavailable for England's second Test defeat by New Zealand pending an inquiry into events that unfolded when they were out celebrating victory in the first Test.

Saracens have carried out their own investigation and are standing by academy player Auvaa, 21, while criticising the England and Wales Cricket Board (ECB) for not involving them in the process.

Rugby.title: Breanne Nicholas still kicking with Canada’s rugby sevens team
Rugby.summary: Breanne Nicholas still kicking with Canada’s rugby sevens team Chatham Daily News
Rugby.body:
Breanne Nicholas prefers to avoid the limelight, but that was impossible this season. The Blenheim veteran became the most-capped player in the history of Canada’s women’s rugby sevens team.

“I wasn’t keeping track of that,” said Nicholas, 32. “My coaches kept reminding me or putting it to the forefront. They definitely celebrated it a lot. “It is a big thing. I know I am one to diminish accomplishments and not make a big deal of it, but it is something to be the most-capped Canadian for sevens for the women.”

Rugby.body:
With the Gibraltar 7s kicking off this Thursday evening at the Europa Sports Complex, Gibraltar will see a full schedule of international sport across its main venues throughout the weekend.

Rugby.summary: Former South Africa loosehead prop Steven Kitshoff has highlighted the importance of Felix Jones ahead of the Springboks' Nations Championship meeting with England at Ellis Park next week.
Rugby.body:
Former South Africa loosehead prop Steven Kitshoff has highlighted the importance of Felix Jones ahead of the Springboks’ Nations Championship meeting with England at Ellis Park next week.

The former Ireland international, who was part of Rassie Erasmus’ backroom staff for their two World Cup triumphs, rejoined the Springbok set-up last year after a year working under Steve Borthwick with England.

Rugby.title: Wood to officiate PWR Final 86 days after giving birth to second child
Rugby.summary: Holly Wood will referee Sunday's 2026 Premiership Women's Rugby Final just 86 after she gave birth to her second child.
Rugby.body:
Holly Wood will referee Sunday’s 2026 Premiership Women’s Rugby Final between Saracens and Trailfinders Women just 86 after she gave birth to her second child. The former Harlequins and Richmond wing, who took up refereeing at the conclusion of her playing career, gave birth to her first child, Huxley, in 2023 and welcomed her daughter, Adaline, into the world in April.

In an interview with PWR, Wood revealed that she learned of her second pregnancy as she prepared to officiate at Women’s Rugby World Cup 2025. Wood opted to keep the pregnancy a secret during the tournament, was a lead official until the 20th week of her pregnancy and was an assistant until the end of her second trimester.

Rugby.title: French heatwave could see Top 14 Final played at record temperatures
Rugby.summary: The Top 14 Final in Paris could be played at record-breaking temperatures this Saturday night as France continues to be gripped by a severe heatwave.
Rugby.body:
The Top 14 Final in Paris could be played at record-breaking temperatures this Saturday night as France continues to be gripped by a severe heatwave. Despite the game kicking off at 9 pm local time, Montpellier and Toulouse could be playing in soaring temperatures, with it forecast to be as hot as 34 degrees at kick-off in Saint-Denis at the Stade de France.

The conditions are similar to the scenes seen in Bilbao during the EPCR Challenge Cup and Investec Champions Cup final last month, where matches kicked off at 31 and 34 degrees.

Rugby.title: Munster duo John Ryan & Niall Scannell: ‘We’ve left those jerseys in a good place’
Rugby.summary: The Ireland front-rowers recall 'surviving the slagging' at Munster, a Rassie rollocking and the highs and lows of their last 13 years together.
Rugby.body:
“You think you’re jumping on to have a nostalgic talk about how much you love the club and all your team-mates, and all that. Then the first thing you get is all your team-mates have actually ratted you out and told a load of stories about you!”

It is Monday after the ‘third or fourth goodbye’ and it is now official – Niall Scannell and John Ryan are former professional rugby players. Both are not long back from school runs and have items on the to-do lists that need ticking off. Are they able to give up an hour of their mornings to look back on their rugby careers? “I could sit here all morning,” Scannell jokes. “Take all the time you need.”

Rugby.summary: Sunday’s AXA Elite 1 final in Biarritz between ASM Romagnat and Stade Bordelais may not be the match-up many had expected, but it is the final the French domestic league deserves.
Rugby.body:
Sunday’s AXA Elite 1 final in Biarritz between ASM Romagnat and Stade Bordelais – a repeat of the 2024 showpiece match – may not be the match-up many had expected, but it is the final the French domestic league deserves.

The good news is that it should be much cooler on the South West coast of France at kick-off than it has been all week, as the heatwave that has blasted the country this week finally relents.

Rugby.title: 'When he stood up in a meeting, the coaches would sit down... we didn't even need a coach'
Rugby.summary: Former South Africa utility back Francois Hougaard has described his former Springboks and Bulls team-mate Fourie du Preez as a "genius", revealing that the legendary scrum-half was effectively a coach on the field during their time together at the Bulls.
Rugby.body:
'When he stood up in a meeting, the coaches would sit down... we didn't even need a coach' Former South Africa utility back Francois Hougaard has described his former Springboks and Bulls team-mate Fourie du Preez as a “genius”, revealing that the legendary scrum-half was effectively a coach on the field during their time together at the Bulls.

The 38-year-old joined the Pretoria franchise after starring for South Africa at age-grade level, despite being warned that his route to the first team would be blocked by Du Preez and fellow scrum-half Heini Adams.

Rugby.title: WXV Global Series: Italy out for World Cup revenge on home soil
Rugby.summary: Italy will be out for Women's Rugby World Cup revenge against South Africa when they host the southern hemisphere side in the WXV Global Series in September.
Rugby.body:
Italy will be out for Women’s Rugby World Cup revenge against South Africa when they host the southern hemisphere side in the WXV Global Series in September. The Italian Rugby Federation have confirmed that their women’s national team will host Japan and South Africa in their home WXV Global Series fixtures this September.

They will host the fixtures across back-to-back weekends in September at Stadio Omero Tognon in Fontanafredda. Italy have played Japan eight times in the two teams’ history. The Europeans have won six of those fixtures, drawn one and lost once.

Rugby.title: Newcastle Red Bulls swoop for ex-Blitz Bok Werner Kok
Rugby.summary: Ulster winger Werner Kok has signed a one-year deal with Newcastle Red Bulls, which could be a sign that the Gallagher PREM outfit are losing their battle to bring Fehi Fineanganofo to Kingston Park next season.
Rugby.body:
Ulster winger Werner Kok has signed a one-year deal with Newcastle Red Bulls, which could be a sign that the Gallagher PREM outfit are losing their battle to bring Fehi Fineanganofo to Kingston Park next season.

Hurricanes speedster Fineanganofo, who broke the Rugby Pacific try-scoring record, is keen to rip up his two-year Red Bulls contract after being named as one of four uncapped players included in Dave Rennie’s first All Blacks squad of the year earlier this week.

Rugby.title: Trailfinders target 'underdog' victory against Saracens
Rugby.summary: Ealing Trailfinders look for a first-ever Premiership Women's Rugby title as they face three-time winners Saracens at the Stoop.
Rugby.body:
Trailfinders' England captain Meg Jones says they are targeting an "underdog victory" in their first Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) final. The West Londoners only joined the PWR in 2023 and had never finished higher than sixth. They secured fourth spot before beating triple champions Gloucester-Hartpury 29-26 at Kingsholm in their semi-final on June 14.

Trailfinders now face three-time champions Saracens at the Stoop this Sunday, with the North London outfit chasing a record fourth title. "I honestly didn't think I'd be here," Jones told BBC Sport. "It would be an underdog story and mean a huge amount to the club. And on the back of the season we've had, it would mean a great deal to me as well."

Rugby.title: Carys Phillips creates unique piece of Barbarians history with Allianz Stadium selection
Rugby.summary: Carys Phillips' selection for the Barbarians this weekend has seen her make a unique piece of history for the world famous invitational side.
Rugby.body:
Carys Phillips‘ selection for the Barbarians this weekend has seen her make a unique piece of history for the world famous invitational side. The 87-cap hooker has followed her father, Rowland, in gaining selection for the side. They are the first father-daughter team to represent the Barbarians.

Phillips, 33, was previously coached by her father for Wales and captained the team during that time. Rowland Phillips is currently the head coach of Wales Deaf Men’s team. Earlier this year, Phillips gained selection to Sean Lynn’s Wales squad for the Guinness Women’s Six Nations, but did not gain selection for any of the team’s five defeats in the tournament. Her last cap came in Wales’ Women’s Rugby World Cup 2025 loss to Fiji.

Rugby.title: Why Alisha and Jasmine Joyce wanted to play domestic rugby in Wales next season
Rugby.summary: In choosing to leave Bristol Bears and return to Wales, Jasmine and Alisha Joyce made a clear decision: family over career. At least in the short term.
Rugby.body:
In choosing to leave Bristol Bears and return to Wales, Jasmine and Alisha Joyce made a clear decision: family over career. At least in the short term. Not in the sense that returning to play on the ‘right’ side of the Severn dramatically hampers their rugby. They will line up in the Celtic Challenge. Both have already done so for the Brython Thunder. International duty, of course, is still in the picture.

The Joyces live in west Wales. In choosing to forego lengthy commutes to Bristol, they will be staying closer to family support now that their young son, Ralphie, has come along. They have chosen family over access to Premiership Women’s Rugby.

Rugby.title: Argubly England's 2 best back-rows to lock horns in Top 14 Final
Rugby.summary: Stade Toulousain and Montpellier HR are just a few days away from finding out which of the two will take the Top 14 trophy home, with several UK players set to be involved in what’s expected to be a game for the ages.
Rugby.body:
Stade Toulousain and Montpellier HR are just a few days away from finding out which of the two will take the Top 14 trophy home, with several UK players set to be involved in what’s expected to be a game for the ages.

With Jack Willis and Blair Kinghorn ready to help Ugo Mola’s Toulouse reach a 25th Bouclier de Brennus – in what would be their 4th consecutive title – Montpellier are bringing Adam Beard, Ali Price and Billy Vunipola to the Stade de France, hellbent on putting an end to Les Rouge et Noir’s reign.

Rugby.title: PWR secures first multi-million pound sponsor deal
Rugby.summary: Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) has secured its first title sponsorship deal which will see over £3m invested into the league.
Rugby.body:
Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) has secured its first paying title sponsor which will see more than £3m invested into the league. The contract with car company Jaecoo will see more than £1m put into the league for each season of the three-year deal.

As part of the deal, from the 2026/27 season the league will be known as the Jaecoo PWR. Speaking to BBC Sport, PWR executive chair Genevieve Shore said the lack of a previous title sponsor investing into the league had been "hanging over PWR" and described the new deal as "momentous and joyful".

Rugby.title: Four uncapped players receive Wales call to face the Barbarians
Rugby.summary: Four players are in line to make their Wales debuts after Steve Tandy named his side to face the Barbarians at Twickenham's Allianz Stadium on Saturday.
Rugby.body:
Four players are in line to make their Wales debuts after Steve Tandy named his side to face the Barbarians at Twickenham’s Allianz Stadium on Saturday. Cardiff loosehead Rhys Barratt, Ospreys tighthead Ben Warren, Dragons lock Ryan Woodman and Dragons back-row Harrison Keddie have all been included among the replacements for the uncapped fixture.

The quartet headline a Wales squad who also welcome back second-row Teddy Williams following injury, while Dewi Lake captains a side packed with established internationals as preparations begin for the Nations Championship.

The quartet headline a Wales squad who also welcome back second-row Teddy Williams following injury, while Dewi Lake captains a side packed with established internationals as preparations begin for the Nations Championship.

Rugby.title: Former Bok hopeful Darrien Landsberg to join Exeter to kickstart Test career
Rugby.summary: Exeter Chiefs are poised to signal Black Knight Entertainment’s takeover at Sandy Park by announcing the arrival of Zimbabwe-born lock Darrien Landsberg from United Rugby Championship side the Lions.
Rugby.body:
Exeter Chiefs are poised to signal Black Knight Entertainment’s takeover at Sandy Park by announcing the arrival of Zimbabwe-born lock Darrien Landsberg from United Rugby Championship side the Lions.

Landsberg, who will be 28 in July, was knocking on Rassie Erasmus’ door last year for an invitation to the Springbok alignment camp taking place in Cape Town, but missed out and has now decided it’s time to head over to Europe.

Rugby.title: 'Almost unbelievable': The England player Borthwick must now back
Rugby.summary: Steve Borthwick must now finally follow the PREM champions’ example this summer, starting with backing Fin Smith to the hilt.
Rugby.body:
Steve Borthwick must now finally follow the PREM champions’ example this summer, starting with backing Fin Smith to the hilt. It’s almost unbelievable that he has just seven Test starts to his name when he is a British & Irish Lion and has led his team to the league title in two of the last three seasons but there is no way he can be overlooked again.

The Northampton man was parachuted into an already struggling side for the final two defeats of a disastrous 2026 Six Nations campaign, and only started once against Fiji in the autumn, that after winning all four games he started in the previous Six Nations.

Rugby.title: Callender to skipper inexperienced Wales team against Barbarians in Twickenham
Rugby.summary: Alex Callender will captain an inexperienced Wales team against the Barbarians on Saturday afternoon at Allianz Stadium.
Rugby.body:
Alex Callender will captain an inexperienced Wales team against the Barbarians on Saturday afternoon at Allianz Stadium. The squad selected by Sean Lynn contains five players without Test match honours for the uncapped fixture. Callender returns to the team after she missed the entirety of the Guinness Women’s Six Nations with injury.

Wales’ fixture against the Barbarians forms part of a doubleheader in West London, with Wales’ men taking on the Barbarians earlier in the afternoon. Two uncapped players are in the starting XV, with wings Amy Williams and Kelsie Webster getting their first taste of international rugby. Nel Metcalfe, who also missed the Women’s Six Nations, completes the back three at full-back.

Rugby.title: The best rugby commentators and pundits of the season
Rugby.summary: The art of commentating and rugby punditry isn't easily learnt but this list of rugby luminaries are the best in the business
Rugby.body:
Rugby players always get the plaudits, but where’s the love for the pundits? For a bit of fun, here are my list of top pundits and commentators from the 2025/26 season – not in any particular order, just some of the best to take the mic*.

Very few players dominate their rugby playing career, retire, and then instantly dominate the comms and punditry world. Warburton did just that. His approach to commentary is exactly as he played – heavy on detail and executed in a way that fans understand. Most players have in-depth knowledge of their position, and the game as a whole, but very few can convey it with brevity. To watch him speak on TV makes it clear why he was captain at every level he played at and why Gatland made him Lions’ captain twice. Warburton’s arrival on the commentary scene came at a time when the rugby public was looking for more detail from their pundits. Gone were the days of just adding a bit of colour to commentary and chatting about the emotional aspects of the game. The public wanted technical analysis and Warburton rewrote the PowerPoint for the whole of rugby comms. Still the best in the business.

Rugby.title: Wales women name five uncapped players for Barbarians
Rugby.summary: Five players will make their senior debuts for Wales when they take on the Barbarians at Twickenham on Saturday (17:00 BST).
Rugby.body:
Five players will make their senior debuts for Wales when they take on the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium on Saturday (17:00 BST). Amy Williams and Kelsie Webster are named in the starting backline, while Stella Orrin, Lucy Isaac and Lily Hawkins are among the replacements.

Kayleigh Powell, who shone at full-back during the Six Nations, gets an opportunity at fly-half, with Lleucu George among a host of Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) players either rested or unavailable for the uncapped fixture.

Rugby.title: Automotive brand ends PWR's two year wait for title sponsor with multi-million pound deal
Rugby.summary: Premiership Women's Rugby have ended their two year search for a title sponsor by securing a multi-million pound and multi-year deal with automotive brand, JAECOO.
Rugby.body:
Premiership Women’s Rugby have ended their two year search for a title sponsor by securing a multi-million pound, multi-year deal with automotive brand, JAECOO. The Chinese manufacturer is one of the fastest growing automotive brands in the United Kingdom and have already established themselves in rugby as the official automotive partner of European Professional Club Rugby [EPCR] and the For the Love of Rugby podcast hosted by ex-England internationals Ben Youngs and Dan Cole.

PWR has been without a title sponsor since the world’s largest insurance company, Allianz, stopped sponsoring the competition in 2024. From the start of the 2026/27 season, the competition will be names JAECOO PWR and the preseason competition will be renamed the JAECOO PWR Next Gen Cup.

Rugby.title: Morgan to make Wales men's return against Barbarians
Rugby.summary: Flanker Jac Morgan is back in Wales' starting line-up for Saturday's uncapped fixture against the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium, Twickenham.
Rugby.body:
Jac Morgan will make his Wales return as a starter against the Barbarians at Allianz Stadium on Saturday (14:00 BST). The British and Irish Lions flanker has been named at open-side for the uncapped fixture in Twickenham.

Morgan returns to the international stage after missing eight Tests because of a dislocated left shoulder suffered when scoring a try against Argentina in boss Steve Tandy's first game last autumn.

Rugby.title: 'It is going to ramp up': Stewart expects big changes in full-time Wallaroos set-up
Rugby.summary: Wallaroos wing Maya Stewart expects there to be plenty of changes in the programme when it is established as a full-time set-up.
Rugby.body:
Wallaroos wing Maya Stewart expects there to be plenty of changes in the programme when it is established as a full-time set-up. Earlier this week Olympic gold medal winning coach Tim Walsh was appointed by Rugby Australia as their Director of Women’s High Performance. Earlier this season the 26-year-old, who is the all-time leading try-scorer for the Wallaroos, spent time with Walsh’s sevens side.

Among the 47-year-old’s responsibilities include launching the Wallaroos as a full-time programme which will be based in Sydney as the side look for improved results in the lead up to their home Women’s Rugby World Cup in 2029.

Rugby.title: Siya Kolisi on the 2018 Bok game against England that 'changed my life'
Rugby.summary: As Siya Kolisi prepares to lead the Springboks for the 72nd time in the Nations Championship opener against England in Johannesburg...
Rugby.body:
As Siya Kolisi prepares to lead the Springboks for the 72nd time in the Nations Championship opener against England in Johannesburg, he has revealed the vital lessons he learnt while inspiring his team to a remarkable comeback victory in his first game as captain against the same opponents in 2018.

The Springboks were 24-3 down at one point but stunned England with a fight back that earned a 42-39 victory. “That 2018 match was a huge learning opportunity for me, and it obviously changed my life. I remember it was very tough and emotional,” Kolisi told local media ahead of the Ellis Park clash on July 4.” I think the emotions got the better of me. I struggled a bit, but the team did well, and I had so many great leaders around me.”

Rugby.title: The Boks and All Blacks prove it's time we think differently about JRLO
Rugby.summary: Phone +54 2122373655. No, its not a number somewhere in Argentina (although you could be forgiven for thinking so with plus 54 the nation’s international phone prefix).
Rugby.body:
No, its not a number somewhere in Argentina (although you could be forgiven for thinking so with plus 54 the nation’s international phone prefix). It’s actually the winning margins from each of the six matches of this year’s Super Rugby finals, with the average gap between the participants throughout the series, an astounding 37.5 points.

It was the most one-sided play-offs series in the competition’s 30-year history, perhaps indicating the need of something new, to at least jazz up the pointy end of the season.

Rugby.title: Cardiff captain Belcher out of Wales summer games
Rugby.summary: Cardiff hooker Liam Belcher is out of Wales' summer matches because of a neck injury.
Rugby.body:
Cardiff hooker Liam Belcher has been ruled out of Wales' summer matches because of a neck injury. Belcher, 30, was one of four hookers in the original squad named by head coach Steve Tandy alongside Cardiff club-mate Evan Lloyd, Scarlets front rower Ryan Elias and captain Dewi Lake.

Wales say Belcher suffered the injury during Cardiff's United Rugby Championship (URC) quarter-final play-off defeat against Stormers at the end of May and he has failed to recover in time.

Rugby.title: Wales lose another player for Nations Championship on eve of BaaBaas
Rugby.summary: Wales hooker Liam Belcher has become the latest player to withdraw from their squad for the Nations Championship campaign due to a neck injury sustained in Cardiff's United Rugby Championship defeat to the Stormers.
Rugby.body:
Wales hooker Liam Belcher has become the latest player to withdraw from their squad for the Nations Championship campaign due to a neck injury sustained in Cardiff’s United Rugby Championship defeat to the Stormers.

The 30-year-old picked up the injury in the second half of the 44-21 loss in Cape Town and has failed to recover in time to feature for his country against Fiji, Argentina and South Africa in July.

Rugby.title: Monaco heads to Moldova with promotion hopes alive in Rugby Sevens Championship
Rugby.summary: Monaco heads to Moldova with promotion hopes alive in Rugby Sevens Championship Monaco Life
Rugby.body:
After making history with a silver medal performance during the opening stage of the Rugby Sevens Trophy Championship in Makarska earlier this month, Monaco’s national sevens team will return to action from 26th to 28th June for the competition’s second stage in Chișinǎu, Moldova.

After already exceeding expectations in Croatia, marking an impressive return to Europe’s second-tier competition and establishing themselves as contenders for promotion, Monaco enters the tournament with its promotion ambitions firmly in place.

Rugby.title: The Welsh hooker preparing to play against Wales
Rugby.summary: Dragons hooker Elliot Dee will start for Barbarians against Wales at the Allianz Stadium on Saturday.
Rugby.body:
While George North has been getting most of the attention this week, it is another Welsh international who starts for Barbarians against Wales on Saturday. Hooker Elliot Dee will be in the Baa-Baas line-up for the uncapped match at the Allianz Stadium in Twickenham, while North will bring down his glittering professional career when he comes off the replacements bench.

The two Welshmen are rooming together in London as they prepare to face the country they have represented for more than a decade. "It's going to be interesting, something I've never done before.

Rugby.title: Wales and Barbarians ready to add to rich history
Rugby.summary: Wales and the Barbarians will add another chapter to their rich history in a double-header in London on Saturday.
Rugby.body:
Wales will add another chapter to the historic fixture against the Barbarians when playing a double-header in Twickenham at the end of the month. Steve Tandy's men will warm up for their Nations Championship Tests when facing the invitational side at 14:00 BST on Saturday, 27 June before Sean Lynn's women take to the field at Allianz Stadium at 17:00 BST.

The teams played a double-header in Cardiff seven years ago and now meet at the home of English rugby in an event hosted by Barbarians. Before the start of another summer of international rugby, BBC Sport Wales looks back at the historic fixture.

Rugby.title: Telusa Veainu: ‘There’s something spiritual, a feeling of home when I go back to the Tonga team’
Rugby.summary: The veteran full-back thought his country "might have run out of players" when he was recalled for the Nations Cup, but is relishing his return.
Rugby.body:
The trio of Telusa Veainu, Logo Mulipola and Semesa Rokoduguni have an affectionate nickname among their younger peers at Doncaster. Having ‘the pensioners’ around, combined age 112, comes with considerable benefits, passing on what it takes to play at the top.

“We’re all mentally the same age [as the younger players], but physically, we are all probably feeling it a lot more, taking a lot more time to recover,” Veainu admits. There will be little opportunity to rest for the 35-year-old this summer. A recall by Tonga to play in the Nations Cup, two years after Veainu’s last cap against Spain, came as a most welcome surprise.

Rugby.title: Beating the Barbarians would be everything
Rugby.summary: Wales head coach Sean Lynn says beating the Barbarians on Saturday would mean everything to his side.
Rugby.body:
Wales head coach Sean Lynn says beating the Barbarians on Saturday would mean everything to his side. Wales head to Allianz Stadium on a nine Test losing streak and will be without some of their Premiership Women's Rugby (PWR) stars for the double-header involving the Wales and Baa-baas men's teams.

It means Lynn will look to Wales' next generation of talent as they prepare to come up against some megastars of the women's game. "You look at 10, 12, 13, they're very exciting for the Barbarians and then you've got Ruby Tui who's going to be explosive," said Lynn.

Rugby.title: 'Devastating' Bermudez signs on for more with Loughborough
Rugby.summary: Canada wing Fancy Bermudez claimed she had found a "sense of family" upon re-signing for Premiership Women's Rugby club Loughborough Lightning.
Rugby.body:
Canada wing Fancy Bermudez claimed she had found a “sense of family” upon re-signing for Premiership Women’s Rugby club Loughborough Lightning. The 24-year-old made 12 appearances in her first season with the East Midlanders and scored 12 tries. Loughborough finished sixth in the PWR table and enjoyed a bright start to the campaign.

Bermudez also spent chunks of the season with Canada Women’s Sevens and competed in HSBC SVNS Series and World Championship competitions. “My main reason for re-signing was the culture and this environment,” Bermudez said. “It has been so refreshing for me; I’ve only been on a select few teams that have given me a sense of family.

Rugby.title: 'She is a natural leader': USA international to exit Trailfinders
Rugby.summary: Experienced USA Women's Eagles back-row Kate Zackary will leave Trailfinders Women after the Premiership Women's Rugby Final this Sunday.
Rugby.body:
Experienced USA Women’s Eagles back-row Kate Zackary will leave Trailfinders Women after the Premiership Women’s Rugby Final this Sunday. The 37-year-old forward, who has represented her country at three Women’s Rugby World Cups, has spent the last three seasons in West London and has been a key player for Barney Maddison’s team.

Zackary joined Trailfinders after three years with Exeter Chiefs in Devon. Upon her arrival in London the forward became a fan favourite and could cap her time with the team with an English league title at the Twickenham Stoop this weekend.
